claim: FEED->STANDUP (disturb) is gated on DISTANCE ONLY (d2 < 1200*1200). The code's own comment brags it dropped 'no facing cone, no global lock (those were my heuristics)'. But the original disturb gate is a 4-way AND: global engage-lock clear AND dist<0x4b0 AND player INSIDE the ±0x200 (45°) facing cone AND a band/floor parity match. Removing the cone, lock and band check is a provable deviation: any feeder rises the instant the player is within 1200u regardless of facing, and ALL feeders disturb simultaneously (the lock that keeps the others eating is gone).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102df8.c:10-15 (also FUN_80103f60.c:10-15, FUN_80103e6c.c:10-14): `if (DAT_800acae7=='\0' && entity[0x1d0]<0x4b0 && func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x200)==0 && DAT_800acad6==entity[0x82]) { row=func_0x8001a780(...)+1; entity[4]=(row)*0x100|1; }` — cone semantics proven in RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001a9cc.c:13-15 (==0 means within ±half).
fix: Gate the FEED->STANDUP transition on all four byte-true conditions: (1) s_engage_lock==0 (the real DAT_800acae7 bit0), (2) d2 < 0x4b0*0x4b0, (3) player inside the ±0x200 (45°) cone — reuse the dot>0 && dot*dot > cos45^2-scaled*d2 test, NOT distance-only, (4) the band/floor match (DAT_800acad6 == zombie band, our actor band field). Then SET s_engage_lock=1 on the chosen riser and clear it on recover/death (FUN_8010305c.c:18 clears DAT_800acae7 &= 0xfe).

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102df8.c:10-14 (mirrored verbatim in FUN_80103f60.c:10-14, FUN_80103e6c.c:10-14, FUN_80101858.c:22-26) is the FEED-row disturb transition and gates it on a 4-way AND: `if (DAT_800acae7=='\0' && entity[0x1d0]<0x4b0 && func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x200)==0 && DAT_800acad6==entity[0x82]) entity[4]=(row+1)*0x100|1;`. Cone semantics proven in RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001a9cc.c:12-15 (returns 0 = inside the ±0x200 half-angle). DAT_800acae7 is a real global engage-lock bit0: set via `|1` by the attack/grab handlers (FUN_8010e7bc.c:53, FUN_8010e87c.c:27, FUN_8010f598.c:14) and cleared via `&0xfe` on recover/death (FUN_8010305c.c:18, FUN_801152cc.c:18). Our code at psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:197 gates FEED->STANDUP on DISTANCE ONLY (`d2 < ZB_AGGRO_DIST*ZB_AGGRO_DIST`) and its own comment (lines 186-188) admits it dropped 'no facing cone, no global lock (those were my heuristics)'. The cone test (`faced`, zombie_common.c:180) and the lock (`s_engage_lock`, lines 81/88/311) already exist in the same file and are used for the IDLE->PURSUE transition at line 209, so omitting them from FEED is a provable, internally-inconsistent deviation, not an accepted arch difference.
verdict.fix_summary: At zombie_common.c:197 replace the distance-only test with the 4-way byte-true gate: (1) s_engage_lock==0, (2) d2 < 0x4b0*0x4b0, (3) player inside the cone (reuse `faced`; ideally retune ZB_COS_CONE2 to the byte-true 0x200=45deg half-angle since func_0x8001a9cc passes 0x200, whereas the current constant encodes ~67deg), (4) the band/floor parity match DAT_800acad6==entity[0x82] (compare player band to the zombie's band field). On the chosen riser set s_engage_lock=1 / ai->claimed=1 (the clear at line 311 already exists), so the other feeders stay eating until that one recovers or dies. This makes feeders rise only when faced+near+unlocked, one at a time, matching the original.

claim: Header doc and the ZB_AGGRO_DIST comment cite the FEED-disturb radius as a single '0x4b0=1200 / aggro dist 0x7d0=2000 + cone'. The IDLE/observer aggro radius is actually 0x7d0 (2000) with cone 0x2c8 (62.6°), while the FEED-disturb radius is 0x4b0 (1200) with cone 0x200 (45°) — two DIFFERENT radii+cones for two different states. Our RE15_ZB_IDLE case uses ZB_AGGRO_DIST=1200 for the idle aggro gate, but the idle handler's gate is dist<2000.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80101364.c:20 `entity[0x1d0] < 2000 && func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x2c8)!=0` (idle, dist 2000, cone 0x2c8) vs FUN_80102df8.c:10-11 (feed-disturb, dist 0x4b0, cone 0x200).
fix: Use dist<0x7d0(2000) for the RE15_ZB_IDLE aggro gate (not 1200), and dist<0x4b0(1200) only for the FEED-disturb gate. Use cone half-angle 0x2c8 (62.6°) for idle and 0x200 (45°) for feed-disturb; our single ZB_COS_CONE2 (~67°) matches neither exactly — split into two cone constants.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80101364.c:20 (idle/observer handler, observer = clip 0x13 set by FUN_80100424.c:42): `if ((*(uint *)(_DAT_800ac784 + 0x1d0) < 2000) && (sVar2 != 0))` -> aggro state 0x701, i.e. proximity-aggro dist = 0x7d0(2000). Our zombie_common.c:209 RE15_ZB_IDLE gate uses `d2 < ZB_AGGRO_DIST*ZB_AGGRO_DIST` with ZB_AGGRO_DIST=1200 (zombie_common.c:31 = 0x4b0, the FEED-disturb radius). FEED-disturb radius 0x4b0 confirmed at FUN_80102df8.c:10 / FUN_80103f60.c:10. Cone helper FUN_8001a9cc.c:13-16 verified: returns 0 = inside cone, nonzero = outside.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation on the DISTANCE: RE15_ZB_IDLE (zombie_common.c:209) must gate on dist<0x7d0(2000), not ZB_AGGRO_DIST=0x4b0(1200). The correct constant ZB_LUNGE_DIST=2000 already exists (zombie_common.c:32, even citing FUN_80101364.c:20) but is wired only to the pursue->attack gate. Fix: at line 209 use 2000 (e.g. ZB_LUNGE_DIST or a new ZB_IDLE_AGGRO_DIST=0x7d0); keep 0x4b0 ONLY for the RE15_ZB_FEED disturb gate (line 197). Shared file, so this fixes PSX+PC. CAVEAT on the claim's cone half: the cone-split proposal is imprecise — FUN_80101364:20 tests cone(0x2c8)!=0 meaning player must be OUTSIDE the cone (turn-then-engage), while FUN_80103f60:11 tests cone(0x200)==0 meaning INSIDE; our single 'faced'(~67 deg, inside) model already only approximates this and is a separate approximation, not the clean 'idle=62.6 deg / feed=45 deg faced' split the claim describes. Recommend fixing the distance (clearly byte-true) and treating the cone semantics as a separate deeper-RE item rather than applying the claim's cone proposal verbatim.

loc: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:39-44 (ZB_STEP=0x200) and the comment claiming 0x474 is not the XZ step
claim: The comment asserts '0x474 at FUN_80102bc8.c:20 is the anim/Y-arm value, NOT the XZ step'. This is provably false: the byte-true mover FUN_800245d8 reads entity[0x8c] as the forward translation magnitude (X-component of a vector rotated by facing entity[0x6a]) and adds it straight to entity world X/Z (+0x34/+0x3c). The walk handler sets entity[0x8c]=0x474 (1140 u/frame); the lunge sets 0x1e (30). Our ZB_STEP=0x200 (512) is therefore a guessed magnitude, not the byte-true value.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800245d8.c:18 `local_30 = entity[0x8c]` then RotMatrixY(entity[0x6a]) + adds result to entity[0x34]/[0x3c]; walk magnitude set at FUN_80102bc8.c:20 `entity[0x8c]=0x474`; lunge magnitude at FUN_80102edc.c:9 `entity[0x8c]=0x1e`; decaying-step example FUN_8010305c.c:15,25-30.
fix: The room1140 savestate path uses clip 0x01/0x02 (a different handler than the 0x16 walk in FUN_80102bc8), so the exact entity[0x8c] for THAT path must be read from a savestate (entity+0x8c) rather than assumed. Replace ZB_STEP=0x200 with the value entity[0x8c] actually holds in the room1140 pursue savestate; do not keep the unproven 0x200. The lunge step in RE15_ZB_ATTACK (0x1e) IS byte-true (FUN_80102edc.c:9) — keep it.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800245d8.c:17,33,45-46 (the XZ mover reads entity[0x8c] as `local_30`, rotates by RotMatrixY(entity[0x6a]+param_1), and adds the result to world X entity[0x34] and world Z entity[0x3c]); RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:20 sets entity[0x8c]=0x474 (walk handler) and :40 calls func_0x800245d8(0) once per anim cycle; RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:9 sets entity[0x8c]=0x1e (lunge). The 0x200 our comment attributes to XZ travel is provably the param_4 of func_0x8001f314 (RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f314.c:1,21 -> FUN_8001f8b4.c:73-74), which is an ANIM keyframe/blend factor fed into FUN_8001ffd8/GTE interpolation indexed by clip entity[0x94] — NOT a collision/XZ step. Our zombie_common.c:39-44 + 223,243 use ZB_STEP=0x200 directly as the per-frame forward XZ magnitude (zb_step_forward), so the value AND its justifying comment are both wrong.
verdict.fix_summary: Could not refute — the cite is real and the deviation is genuine. ZB_STEP=0x200 is a guessed XZ magnitude with a provably-false comment (0x200 is the FUN_8001f314 anim-blend factor, not an XZ step). The byte-true XZ travel is entity[0x8c] applied via FUN_800245d8 ONCE PER ANIM CYCLE (not every frame): the walk handler FUN_80102bc8 uses 0x474, the lunge FUN_80102edc uses 0x1e. The room1140 pursue path uses clip 0x01/0x02 (handlers FUN_801016f0/FUN_8010d79c, which index a different anim table entity[0x84]/[0x16c] and preset entity[0x8c] from a parent state — FUN_8010d79c.c:63 shows 0xf0 in one branch), so the exact constant for THAT path is not statically fixed. FIX: (1) drop the false comment; (2) replace the literal 0x200 with the actual entity[0x8c] value read live from a room1140 pursue savestate (savestate_1..6.sav exist; read entity+0x8c at the entity pool 0x800acc2c), and apply XZ translation on anim-cycle completion rather than every frame to match FUN_800245d8's call site. Keep the lunge step 0x1e (FUN_80102edc.c:9) which IS byte-true.

claim: The bite/grab is fired on a fixed timer frame (ai->timer >= ZB_LUNGE_HIT, '~half of 65f', marked TUNABLE). The original fires the grab on the LUNGE clip's ANIMATION-DONE flag (0x2000) — i.e. at clip end, not a hardcoded mid-frame. Our own comment at lines 48-50 even states this, then the code contradicts it. The keyframe SE func_0x800453d0(9) and the grab-attach (FUN_8010383c) are both driven by the anim-done branch, not a frame counter.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:25-34 — grab path taken only when `entity[0x8f]=='\0' && (animflags & 0x2000)!=0 && (entity[0x1d8]&0x40)==0` -> FUN_8010383c; FUN_8010383c.c:23 `func_0x800453d0(9)` (bite/grab SE) + grab-attach. The 0x2000 is the clip-finished flag (same flag tested by func_0x8001f314 returns elsewhere).
fix: Fire the bite/grab when the lunge clip reaches its final frame (clip-done), gated additionally by the entity[0x8f]==0 'not-yet-bitten' latch and the 0x40/0x2000 flags, instead of `ai->timer>=32`. Drop the ZB_LUNGE_HIT magic constant.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:25-34 (grab branch taken only when entity[0x8f]=='\0' && (entity[0x168]&0x2000)!=0 [clip-done] && (entity[0x1d8]&0x40)==0 -> FUN_8010383c, else FUN_80109470 keeps lunging); FUN_8010383c.c:23 func_0x800453d0(9) = the bite/grab SE + grab-attach. Our deviating code: zombie_common.c:266,275 fires on ai->timer>=ZB_LUNGE_HIT with ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32 (zombie_common.c:53, a mid-clip '~half of 65f' magic). 0x2000 is the clip-finished flag, consistently tested across the overlay (e.g. FUN_801016f0.c:32, FUN_801019f8.c:78) and set by the clip-advance func_0x80019700(0x2000,...).
verdict.fix_summary: Fire the bite/grab when the lunge clip reaches its final frame (clip-done), not at a hardcoded mid-frame. Replace the `ai->timer >= ZB_LUNGE_HIT` trigger (zombie_common.c:275) with a clip-finished test: gate on the not-yet-bitten latch (mirror of entity[0x8f]==0, our ai->hit_done==0) AND the lunge clip having completed — i.e. ai->timer >= ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES (65, the real EDD clip 0x09 length) rather than 32. Drop the ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32 magic constant. The lunge-step loop should also run until clip end (timer<=ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES, not <=ZB_LUNGE_HIT) so the zombie keeps closing/reaching for the whole clip like FUN_80109470 does, then bites on completion. (If our actor anim layer exposes a real per-clip anim-done flag, prefer testing that directly to mirror entity[0x168]&0x2000 exactly.)

claim: Per-state frame durations are hand-entered constants (59/65/16) labelled byte-true-from-EDD, but the original does NOT use fixed frame counts to leave these states — it advances the clip and transitions on the clip-finished return of func_0x8001f314 (the 0x200/anim-done flag). E.g. head-up FUN_801040e8 transitions when func_0x8001f314(...,0x200) returns nonzero; rise/feed cycle same. Using a fixed timer is a structural deviation that can desync from the real clip lengths.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801040e8.c:25-29,43-45 (phase advances on `func_0x8001f314(...,0x200)!=0`, not a frame count); FUN_80104250.c:35-37 same; FUN_80103d48.c:30-32 feed loop driven by func_0x8001f314 return.
fix: Drive STANDUP/PURSUE/ATTACK/RECOVER transitions off the actual clip-finished signal (our anim end-of-clip), mirroring func_0x8001f314's 0x200 return, rather than the fixed ZB_RISE_FRAMES/ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES/ZB_RECOVER_FRAMES timers. The 59/65 EDD lengths can stay as a fallback but the transition condition should be clip-done.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801040e8.c:25-29 (state byte +6 advances to 2 only when func_0x8001f314(...,0,0x200)!=0); FUN_801040e8.c:43-45 and FUN_80104250.c:35-37 and FUN_80103d48.c:30-32 (entity[6] += func_0x8001f314 return). The mechanism is proven in the stepper RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f3bc.c:89-95: bVar3=entity[0x95]+1; entity[0x95]=bVar3; return (param_4 <= bVar3) with entity[0x95]=0 on wrap — i.e. func_0x8001f314 returns nonzero exactly when the per-clip frame counter reaches the clip length (clip-done), not at any fixed count. Our deviation: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:228,295,310 gate STANDUP/ATTACK/RECOVER on independent hand-entered timers (ZB_RISE_FRAMES=59/ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES=65/ZB_RECOVER_FRAMES=16, defined :51-54 and self-labelled TUNABLE at :18-20). Our engine already has the real signal (re15_actor.h:82 anim_frame = entity[0x95] mirror, wraps per re15_actor.h:170-174; per-clip length clip->frame_count in anim_select_common.c:34,46), so the clip-wrap condition is available but unused.
verdict.fix_summary: Drive the STANDUP->PURSUE, ATTACK-end, and RECOVER-end transitions off the actual clip-finished signal, mirroring FUN_8001f3bc's entity[0x95]>=clip_length wrap (return of func_0x8001f314 with the 0x200 selector), instead of the fixed ZB_RISE_FRAMES/ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES/ZB_RECOVER_FRAMES timers. Concretely: add a helper that returns (z->anim_frame + 1) >= clip->frame_count for the actor's current motion clip (clip->frame_count already available via anim_select_common.c / emd_common.c), and replace the `++ai->timer >= ZB_*_FRAMES` tests at zombie_common.c:228/295/310 with that clip-done test. Keep the 59/65 EDD lengths only as a safety fallback. Note: ZB_LUNGE_HIT (the mid-grab bite frame at :266/275) is a separate keyframe event, not a state-leave condition, so it can stay frame-indexed (the overlay fires the bite via func_0x800453d0 on its own keyframe, FUN_80102edc); only the state-LEAVE conditions must become clip-driven.

claim: Turn-to-player slew rate 0x10 per frame is applied UNIFORMLY in every moving state. Byte-true: the walk/pursue uses func_0x8001aa68(target,0x10) (rate 0x10) — correct — but other states use different slews (e.g. the post-bite/recover uses func_0x8001aac4(...,0x20) = rate 0x20, and the head-up uses 0x20). Applying a flat 0x10 to recover/standup understates the real turn rate in those states.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:54 `func_0x8001aa68(...,0x10)` (walk slew 0x10) vs FUN_801040e8.c:41 `func_0x8001aac4(...,0x20)` and FUN_8010383c.c:25 `func_0x8001aac4(...,0x20)` (head-up/grab slew 0x20).
fix: Keep ZB_TURN_RATE=0x10 for PURSUE (byte-true) but use 0x20 for the STANDUP/head-up and grab/recover turn (per FUN_801040e8.c:41, FUN_8010383c.c:25). Split into ZB_TURN_RATE_WALK=0x10 and ZB_TURN_RATE_ENGAGE=0x20.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801040e8.c:41 `func_0x8001aac4(targetX@0x1bc,targetZ@0x1be,0x20)` is the HEAD-UP/rise handler (FUN_801040e8.c:18,37 sets clip 0x94=0x27+/0x29 = our ZB_CLIP_RISE/STANDUP) and turns toward the live player point at rate 0x20. RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_8010383c.c:25 `func_0x8001aac4(...,0x20)` is the GRAB/BITE handler (FUN_8010383c.c:23 `func_0x800453d0(9)` = the bite SE = our ATTACK/-3 bite) and also turns at rate 0x20. Both map to our states (zombie_common.c:72 ZB_CLIP_RISE=0x29, :74 ZB_CLIP_ATTACK=0x09) which we slew with ZB_TURN_RATE=0x10 (zombie_common.c:43-44, applied at :219 STANDUP and :264 ATTACK). FUN_8001aac4 (RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001aac4.c:9-22) clamps the per-frame yaw step at 0x6a to +/-param_3, the SAME semantics as our zb_turn_toward clamp (zombie_common.c:116-117) — so 0x20 vs 0x10 is a true 2x understatement in those states. CONTRAST: the lunge-APPROACH/pursue (FUN_80102edc.c:17 `func_0x8001aac4(...,0x10)`) genuinely uses 0x10, so PURSUE at 0x10 is byte-true and must stay. (The claim's FUN_80102bc8.c:54 cite is a red herring — that uses func_0x8001aa68 toward a STORED angle (byte0x90&0xf0)<<4, not the player — but it does not affect the verdict since the 0x20 cites for head-up/grab stand on their own.)
verdict.fix_summary: Split the single ZB_TURN_RATE=0x10 into ZB_TURN_RATE_WALK=0x10 (PURSUE — byte-true per FUN_80102edc.c:17) and ZB_TURN_RATE_ENGAGE=0x20. Apply 0x20 to the STANDUP slew (zombie_common.c:219, per FUN_801040e8.c:41) and the ATTACK/grab slew (zombie_common.c:264, per FUN_8010383c.c:25); the post-grab RECOVER (zombie_common.c:309) also turns at 0x20 per FUN_801035e8.c:42. Keep PURSUE (:235) and the cone-based IDLE at 0x10 (IDLE has no cited turn handler, so leave it unless a savestate proves otherwise).

claim: The file header cites non-existent / wrong handlers and clips as byte-true: 'stand-up exec FUN_80104348: clip 0x18 (rise) -> 0x19 (settle)' — there is NO FUN_80104348 in the overlay, and the real head-up handlers (FUN_801040e8/FUN_80104250) write clip 0x29, not 0x18/0x19. The header enum comments still say 'clip 0x18 rise / 0x19 settle / 0x0C walk / 0x1F lunge' which do not match the savestate-verified room1140 clips (rise 0x29, walk 0x01, bite 0x09). Stale doc invites future wrong edits.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801040e8.c:37 and FUN_80104250.c:27 write clip 0x29 for head-up (no FUN_80104348 exists; verified via Glob of the STAGE1 overlay dir). Feed clip = entity[5]+6 FUN_80103d48.c:20.
fix: Correct the header/comment citations: rise/head-up handler = FUN_801040e8/FUN_80104250 clip 0x29; feed handler = FUN_80103d48 (clip entity[5]+6 => 0x27/0x28); lunge clip 0x1a/0x1f handlers FUN_80102edc/FUN_801074b0. Remove the fabricated FUN_80104348 and the 0x18/0x19/0x0C references that no room1140 handler writes.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801040e8.c:37 and FUN_80104250.c:27 both write *(undefined1 *)(_DAT_800ac784 + 0x94) = 0x29 for the head-up/rise from feeding; FUN_80103d48.c:20 writes entity[0x94] = entity[5]+6 (feed = 0x27/0x28). There is NO FUN_80104348 in the overlay (Glob found none; nearest is FUN_80104340.c which is a non-decompilable stub at a different address 0x80104340, not 0x80104348, with no body proving any clip). The fabricated handler FUN_80104348 + clips 0x18/0x19/0x0C/0x1F are cited in zombie_common.c:11,13 and re15_zombie.h:16-17,31,33, contradicting both the disasm (0x29) and the code's OWN savestate-verified constants (zombie_common.c:51,70-74: ZB_CLIP_RISE=0x29, ZB_CLIP_WALK=0x01, ZB_CLIP_ATTACK=0x09).
verdict.fix_summary: Comment-only doc fix (executable constants are already correct/byte-true). In zombie_common.c:4-21 and re15_zombie.h:16-17,31,33 replace the fabricated 'FUN_80104348: clip 0x18 (rise) -> 0x19 (settle)' and the '0x18 rise / 0x19 settle / 0x0C walk / 0x1F lunge' references with the verified handlers/clips: head-up/rise = FUN_801040e8.c:37 / FUN_80104250.c:27 writing clip 0x29; feed = FUN_80103d48.c:20 clip entity[5]+6 (0x27/0x28); pursue walk = 0x01, bite/grab lunge = 0x09 (savestate-verified). Lunge/attack handlers are FUN_80102edc / FUN_801074b0 (both exist). Remove the non-existent FUN_80104348 reference so future edits don't regress the (already-correct) ZB_CLIP_* constants back to 0x18/0x19/0x0C/0x1F.

claim: The feeding chew SE is fired on a fabricated cadence `(ai->timer + i*23) % 69 == 0x28` with a per-zombie phase offset i*23. The original fires func_0x800453d0(3) when the feed clip's keyframe byte entity[0x95] equals '(' (0x28) — a real animation keyframe event, not a modulo-69 timer with a synthetic per-index offset.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80103d48.c:25-27 `if (entity[0x95]=='(') func_0x800453d0(3);` (keyframe-driven, not timer%69).
fix: Drive the chew SE off the actual feed-clip keyframe (our equivalent of entity[0x95]==0x28) instead of the timer%69 + i*23 heuristic. If our anim model exposes per-clip keyframe events, key off frame 0x28 of clip 0x27; the i*23 desync hack should be removed.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80103d48.c:25-28 -- `if (*(char*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x95)=='(') func_0x800453d0(3);` immediately followed by the animation player `func_0x8001ad68(_DAT_800ac784, entity[0x170], entity[0x174])`. entity[0x95] is the per-clip KEYFRAME-EVENT byte (init=0 at line 21, written each frame by func_0x8001ad68 from the clip stream), NOT a timer. Same pattern across the overlay: FUN_801023d8.c:29 ('('), FUN_80102bc8.c:27 ('F'), FUN_80102edc.c:22 ('#'/'P'), FUN_801074b0.c:26/34 ('\a'/'#') -- all ASCII keyframe markers. Our zombie_common.c:196 substitutes `((ai->timer++ + i*23) % 69) == 0x28`, and its own comment (lines 192-195) admits this is a free-running-uint8 work-around with a fabricated per-zombie i*23 phase offset.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation. Original chew SE is keyframe-driven: fires func_0x800453d0(3) when the feed clip's event byte entity[0x95]==0x28 ('('), set by the engine anim player func_0x8001ad68 from the clip's embedded keyframe-event stream. Our code fakes it with (ai->timer + i*23) % 69 == 0x28 -- a guessed 69f clip length plus a synthetic per-index i*23 desync with no disasm basis (each zombie's entity[0x95] advances on its own independent anim clock; no fixed +23 stagger exists). Fix: expose the feed-clip's per-frame keyframe-event byte in our enemy anim model and gate zb_play_se(-2) on event-byte==0x28 of clip 0x27, removing both the %69 modulus and the i*23 offset.

claim: ZB_CLIP_WALK = 0x01 used for the ENTIRE PURSUE state. The overlay's active walk/turn FSM does write clip 1 (FUN_80102bc8 state-0, FUN_801016f0 state-0), but the actual close/approach ROWs write entity[0x94] = entity[0x1d4] (a per-enemy clip slot filled at RUNTIME), and the savestate showed this resolving to TWO distinct clips for the room1140 zombie: 0x01 (close/ROW3) and 0x02 (approach-reach/ROW2). Our code collapses both to 0x01, so the reach-forward approach clip 0x02 is never played. The 0x01 base is disasm-confirmed; the 0x02 variant cannot be cited as a static literal because entity[0x1d4] is runtime-filled (dispatch tables are runtime-filled per the overlay), only the savestate distinguishes them.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:48 (`...0x94) = 1;` state-0) and FUN_80102814.c:17,38 (clip=entity[0x1d4]; sets entity[5]=2 -> ROW2 approach) and FUN_801025c8.c:15 (clip=entity[0x1d4])
fix: Distinguish the approach (ROW2, reach-forward clip 0x02) from the close-shamble (ROW3, clip 0x01) when within an outer ring but still > grab dist, e.g. play 0x02 while dist is in (ZB_GRAB_DIST, ZB_LUNGE_DIST] and the player is in the forward cone, 0x01 otherwise. Confirm the exact 0x02 trigger ring from the savestate distances (RE15_AILOG dump) before wiring it; entity[0x1d4]=0x01/0x02 is only recoverable dynamically, so cite the savestate, not a static literal. Visually low-impact, so leave if it does not improve the validated look.

verdict.proof: Disasm cites verified real and accurate: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102814.c:17 (`*(undefined1*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x94)=*(undefined1*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x1d4);`) and :38 (`*(undefined1*)(_DAT_800ac784+5)=2;`); FUN_801025c8.c:15 (`0x94=0x1d4`); FUN_80102bc8.c:48 (`*(undefined1*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x94)=1;` in the bVar1==0 branch). entity[0x1d4] is the runtime clip slot (=0x02 in every room1140 zombie slot per savestate). Savestate ground truth (entity pool @0x800acc2c, stride 0x1f4, engaging zombie slot1 @0x800ace20): savestate_3 clip=0x01 (st5=03/st6=03), savestate_4 clip=0x02 (st5=03/st6=05), HASH-881C08B8082E53B6_1 shows slot1 clip=0x02 AND slot2 clip=0x01 SIMULTANEOUSLY. So the original emits BOTH 0x01 (ROW3 close-shamble) and 0x02 (ROW2/0x1d4 approach-reach) during pursue, while our zombie_common.c:70/:234 (`ZB_CLIP_WALK=0x01`) collapses the whole PURSUE state to 0x01 and never plays 0x02 (0x02 only appears as the rejected old ZB_CLIP_ATTACK comment at :75).
verdict.fix_summary: REAL deviation, but do NOT wire a fix yet (matches the claim's "uncertain"). Confirmed: the original distinguishes a reach-forward approach clip 0x02 (= entity[0x1d4], copied by the ROW2 handler FUN_80102814:17 / FUN_801025c8:15) from the close shamble clip 0x01 (FUN_80102bc8:48), and our single ZB_CLIP_WALK=0x01 drops 0x02 — savestate-proven (slot1 cycles 0x27->0x29->0x01->0x02->0x09; HASH shows 0x01 and 0x02 on two zombies at once). The blocker to a byte-true fix: the 0x01-vs-0x02 selector is the runtime sub-state byte entity[6] (st6=03 vs 05), driven by RUNTIME-FILLED dispatch tables — NOT a static distance literal, and the savestate player positions cannot be reliably located to derive a trigger ring (player reads as (0,0) in 3 of 4 relevant saves). So the proposed "play 0x02 in (GRAB,LUNGE] ring + forward cone" is itself a GUESS at the trigger and would violate the cite-or-stop rule. Next RE step before any code: RE the st6 (offset-6) sub-state machine that selects FUN_80102814(ROW2) vs FUN_801025c8(ROW3), or capture a sweep of savestates with a locatable player to map dist->clip. Visually low-impact, so leaving it (no 0x02 reach clip) does not worsen the validated look.

claim: The four per-state FRAME-DURATION constants ZB_RISE_FRAMES=59, ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES=65, ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32, ZB_RECOVER_FRAMES=16 are guessed magic numbers (the source comments even say 'mid-grab (~half of 65f)' and the header banner lists 'the per-state frame durations' as TUNABLE). The original does NOT count fixed frames: every state transition fires when the EDD clip reaches a keyframe whose flag word is set (func_0x8001f314 walks the clip keyframes; the handlers test that keyframe's flag bits 0x2000 / 0x10000) AND an entity[0x8f] anim-done countdown that the data sets to literal 0xf(15)/7. The lunge bite is NOT a fixed 32-frame midpoint; it is gated on the 0x2000 / 0x10000 keyframe flags of the lunge clip.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:13 (entity[0x8f]=0xf), :16 (keyframe flag & 0x10000 triggers turn), :26 (& 0x2000 gates the lunge bite branch), :31 (& 0x40); RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:19,50 (entity[0x8f]=7 walk duration); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f314.c:8,15,17 (frame index entity[0x95] into clip entity[0x94], keyframe flag dispatch)
fix: Drive state transitions off the clip keyframe flags, not hardcoded frame counts: STANDUP/RECOVER should advance entity[0x8f] (init 0xf and 7 respectively per FUN_80102edc.c:13 / FUN_80102bc8.c:19) and transition when it underflows to 0 (FUN_80102edc.c:25 `if entity[0x8f]=='\0'`); the ATTACK bite must fire when the lunge clip's current keyframe has bit 0x2000 set (FUN_80102edc.c:26) rather than at timer>=32. Replace ZB_RISE_FRAMES/ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES/ZB_LUNGE_HIT/ZB_RECOVER_FRAMES with the entity[0x8f] countdown seeds 0xf and 7 plus the keyframe-flag check.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:13 seeds entity[0x8f]=0xf, :25 transitions only `if entity[0x8f]=='\0'`, :26 `& 0x2000` keyframe-flag gates the lunge/bite branch (:31-33 -> FUN_8010383c), not a frame count; FUN_80102edc.c:16 `& 0x10000` keyframe flag drives the turn. FUN_8010383c.c:23 `func_0x800453d0(9)` = the bite sound, reached ONLY via the 0x2000 keyframe-flag branch. RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f3bc.c:78 `entity[0x8f] = entity[0x8f] - 1` (per-frame inter-keyframe countdown), :89-94 advances/wraps frame index entity[0x95] at the clip's keyframe count (param_4) and FUN_8001f314.c:16 stores the current keyframe ptr into entity[0x168] whose flag word the handlers test. FUN_80102bc8.c:19,50 seed entity[0x8f]=7 (walk). Our code instead: zombie_common.c:228 `++ai->timer >= ZB_RISE_FRAMES(59)`, :266/:275 `ai->timer <= / >= ZB_LUNGE_HIT(32)`, :295 `>= ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES(65)`, :310 `>= ZB_RECOVER_FRAMES(16)` -- hardcoded frame counts (defs at zombie_common.c:51-54, comment ':53 "~half of 65f"', banner :18-20 lists 'per-state frame durations' as TUNABLE).
verdict.fix_summary: Drive state transitions off the EDD clip data, not hardcoded frame counts. (1) Replace the ai->timer-vs-ZB_*_FRAMES tests with: a per-keyframe sub-step countdown seeded to 0xf (STANDUP/rise) and 7 (walk/recover) per FUN_80102edc.c:13 / FUN_80102bc8.c:19,50, decremented once per anim-advance frame (mirror FUN_8001f3bc.c:78), and transition the state when it underflows to 0 (FUN_80102edc.c:25 `if entity[0x8f]=='\0'`). (2) The ATTACK bite (ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32 "~half") must fire when the current lunge keyframe's flag word has bit 0x2000 set (FUN_80102edc.c:26 -> FUN_8010383c), not at a fixed timer>=32; read the keyframe flag from the clip the same way FUN_8001f314/FUN_8001f3bc:89 advance entity[0x95] and wrap at the EDD keyframe count. (3) The clip end (re-pursue/recover) should key off the frame-index wrap (param_4 in FUN_8001f3bc.c:91), i.e. the real EDD clip length of our extracted 0x09/0x29/0x16 clips, instead of literal 59/65/16. This removes all four guessed magic numbers. Note: independent of the savestate-corrected clip IDs (0x01/0x09/0x29) -- only the timing mechanism changes.

claim: The aggro/disturb FACING CONE is wrong. (a) The FEED->STANDUP transition uses DISTANCE ONLY (`d2 < ZB_AGGRO_DIST*ZB_AGGRO_DIST`, no cone) — the source comment explicitly says 'DISTANCE ONLY, no facing cone'. The original disturb handler additionally REQUIRES the player to be inside a ±45° cone of the zombie's facing (func_0x8001a9cc(&player,0x200)==0). (b) The IDLE->PURSUE cone uses ZB_COS_CONE2=2563201 which is cos^2*4096^2 for ~±67°, not the byte-true ±45° (half-angle 0x200 = 512/4096*360 = 45°).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102df8.c:11 and FUN_80103f60.c:11 (`func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x200), iVar1 == 0` required for the rise transition); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001a9cc.c:8-16 (returns 0 only when player within ±param_2/4096*360 of entity facing entity[0x6a]); aggro dist FUN_80102df8.c:10 (`entity[0x1d0] < 0x4b0`)
fix: Add the ±45° cone gate to FEED->STANDUP and set ZB_COS_CONE2 = cos^2(45°)*4096^2 = 0.5*16777216 = 8388608 (currently 2563201). i.e. require `faced` (dot>0 && dot*dot > 8388608*d2) AND `d2 < 1200^2` before rising, matching func_0x8001a9cc(...,0x200)==0 with the disturb-distance test.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102df8.c:10-12 and FUN_80103f60.c:10-12 (rise/disturb requires `*(uint*)(ent+0x1d0) < 0x4b0` AND `func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x200)==0` AND same-band `DAT_800acad6==ent[0x82]`); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001a9cc.c:10-16 decode (uVar2=(0x200+(angleToPlayer-ent[0x6a]))&0xfff; returns 0 iff uVar2<0x400, i.e. player within ±0x200=±45° of facing); our miss at psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:197 (FEED->STANDUP gated on d2<ZB_AGGRO_DIST^2 ONLY, no cone) and :46 (ZB_COS_CONE2=2563201 = cos^2(~67°)*4096^2, not cos^2(45°)*4096^2=8388608).
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation on both points. (a) FEED->STANDUP (zombie_common.c:197) must add the ±45° facing-cone gate that the original rise handlers FUN_80102df8/FUN_80103f60 require: rise only when d2<0x4b0^2 AND `faced` (dot>0 && dot*dot > 8388608*d2), matching func_0x8001a9cc(&player,0x200)==0. The source comment 'DISTANCE ONLY, no facing cone' is wrong — the disasm clearly has the cone. (b) ZB_COS_CONE2 must change 2563201 -> 8388608 (= cos^2(45°)*4096^2 = 0.5*16777216), which fixes both the FEED->STANDUP cone and the IDLE->PURSUE cone (line 209) to the byte-true ±45° (the 0x200 half-angle of func_0x8001a9cc). NOTE for full byte-trueness: the original also gates on same band `ent[0x82]==player_band`; in the flat room1140 briefing room this is always true so it is not room1140-observable, but a complete fix should include it for multi-room. I verified the cites are verbatim and independently decoded func_0x8001a9cc to confirm the ±45° (0x200/0x1000) semantics — could not refute.

claim: ZB_GRAB_DIST=350 (the lunge/approach STOP ring) has no disassembly basis — the source comment calls it 'prevents the fly-through-the-player oscillation', i.e. an engine-added anti-overshoot heuristic. The original overlay never translates the body 'through' the player via a free slide that needs a stop ring: movement is collision-constrained func_0x8001f314(...,0x200) steps and the bite fires on the anim keyframe (0x2000), so there is no 350-unit constant anywhere in the zombie handlers.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:32,52 (collision move step 0x200, no stop-ring); FUN_80102edc.c:26 (bite gated on keyframe flag 0x2000, not on a numeric reach); grep of the STAGE1 overlay shows no 350/0x15e constant in any zombie handler
fix: Tie the lunge/bite to the lunge clip's 0x2000 keyframe flag and rely on collision constraint for stopping (FUN_80102edc.c:26), removing the synthetic 350u ring. If a contact radius is genuinely needed for the collapsed model, it must be derived from the player+zombie collision-cylinder radii (entity collision fields), not a guessed 350.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:26,31 (bite/grab gated on anim keyframe flag 0x2000 via entity[0x168], NOT a numeric reach) + FUN_8010383c.c:23 (grab spawn func_0x800453d0(9) fires from that keyframe path, no distance test); FUN_80102bc8.c:31-33 (movement is collision/anim-constrained func_0x8001f314 return-checked, no stop ring) and FUN_8001f314.c:8-22 (this func is the anim-keyframe advancer setting entity[0x168], the 0x200/0x100 arg is the anim step, confirming the body translates by collision-resolved root motion). All zombie state-transition distances use the precomputed entity[0x1d0] field with byte-true gates only (FUN_80102df8.c:10 <0x4b0; FUN_80101364.c:20 <2000); grep of the entire STAGE1 overlay shows NO 350/0x15e constant in any zombie handler.
verdict.fix_summary: ZB_GRAB_DIST=350 (zombie_common.c:37, used at lines 222-224,244,268,271,297) is a confirmed non-byte-true engine-added anti-overshoot heuristic — no such constant exists in the overlay. The original never slides the body "through" the player on a free vector: XZ travel is the lunge clip's collision-resolved root motion (func_0x8001f314 anim advance + func_0x800245d8 / band shift), so the player collision cylinder itself stops the approach, and the bite/grab fires on the lunge clip's 0x2000 keyframe flag (FUN_80102edc.c:26 -> FUN_8010383c grab spawn), not on a numeric reach. Byte-true fix: drop the synthetic 350u ring; cap/stop forward translation via re15_collision_constrain against the player+zombie collision-cylinder radii (read from the entity collision fields), and trigger the bite on the lunge clip's keyframe rather than the ZB_GRAB_DIST/ZB_LUNGE_HIT timer proxy. NOTE: this verdict overturns the audit's 'uncertain' category — the cites are real and the disasm positively proves the deviation; it is a confirmed deviation, not merely suspected. The cites in our source comments (the '350 prevents fly-through' comment) self-document it as a heuristic.

claim: VERIFIED OK. Turn-rate 0x10/frame, collision step 0x200/frame, and lunge speed entity[0x8c]=0x1e all match the overlay exactly.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:54 (func_0x8001aa68(...,0x10) turn slew = 0x10); FUN_80102bc8.c:32,52 (func_0x8001f314(...,0,0x200) XZ step = 0x200); FUN_80102edc.c:9 (entity[0x8c]=0x1e lunge speed)
fix: none

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:20 (lunge XZ step = func_0x8001f314(dir@0x170,dir@0x174,0,0x100) = 0x100/frame); FUN_80102edc.c:9 (entity[0x8c]=0x1e is a SEPARATE field, NOT fed to func_0x8001f314); FUN_80102bc8.c:32,52 (walk step 0x200, c.f. our ZB_STEP ok); FUN_80102bc8.c:54 (turn 0x10, c.f. our ZB_TURN_RATE ok). Our deviation: zombie_common.c:269 uses `step = 0x1e` as the per-frame XZ translation in RE15_ZB_ATTACK.
verdict.fix_summary: The audit's verdict is only PARTLY right. Turn-rate 0x10 (zombie_common.c:43) and walk step 0x200 (line 39) ARE byte-true — both literally appear as the 4th arg of func_0x8001f314 / arg of func_0x8001aa68 in FUN_80102bc8.c (lines 32/52/54). But the 'lunge speed entity[0x8c]=0x1e' sub-claim is misapplied. In the overlay's attack handler FUN_80102edc.c, 0x1e is written to entity[0x8c] (line 9) — a non-XZ anim/timer field, never passed to the collision-move — while the ACTUAL per-frame XZ translation during the lunge is the literal 0x100 in func_0x8001f314(...,0,0x100) at FUN_80102edc.c:20. Our code at zombie_common.c:269 uses `step = 0x1e` as the lunge XZ step, so the zombie approaches ~8.5x slower than byte-true during the attack (256→30 u/frame before the GRAB-ring cap). Byte-true fix: in RE15_ZB_ATTACK use step = 0x100 (the FUN_80102edc.c:20 literal), not 0x1e; keep the existing `if (step > dist - ZB_GRAB_DIST) step = dist - ZB_GRAB_DIST` clamp. Leave ZB_TURN_RATE/ZB_STEP as-is (those parts of the audit are correct).

claim: Enemy SE resolution BYPASSES the snd1 EDT descriptor table and hardcodes (program=0, tone=ID+1). The original resolves an SE situation ID through the snd1/program-3 EDT: func_0x800453d0(ID) reads EDT[ID] (4-byte descriptor at DAT_800ac778+0x14), takes program = descriptor[1]&0x7f and tone = descriptor[2]>>4, then indexes the VAB. The (program,tone) mapping is fully DATA-DRIVEN by the room snd1 EDT, not a fixed program 0 / tone=ID+1. Our code even loads s_em_edt = rdt->snd_edt[1] (audio_psx.c:214) but never uses it for resolution (s_em_edt is dead — only assigned at lines 168/202/214, read nowhere).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800453d0.c:28 (pbVar9 = *(int*)(DAT_800ac778+0x14) + (param_1&0xff)*4) and :32-37 (bVar1=pbVar9[2]; uVar12=bVar1>>4 = tone; bVar4=pbVar9[1] = program); cf. FUN_80045024.c:50-54 case 3 confirming DAT_800ac778+0x14 = the program-3/snd1 EDT, mirroring the byte-true footstep path re15_se_resolve(snd_edt, vab, index) in vab_common.c:161-187
fix: Resolve enemy SE exactly like the footstep path: tn = NULL; vag = re15_se_resolve(rdt->snd_edt[1], &s_em_vab, ID, &tn) where ID is the raw situation ID (detect=5, feed=3, bite=2, kick=7, got-hit=6, grab=9). Drop the em_resolve_pt(prog0, ID+1) hardcode. re15_se_resolve already reads program=e[1]&0x7f and tone=e[2]>>4 byte-true. Pass the situation ID, not ID+1, and not a fixed program.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800453d0.c:28 (pbVar9 = *(int*)(DAT_800ac778+0x14) + (param_1&0xff)*4 -> EDT[ID], NOT ID+1); :38+:40 (bVar4=pbVar9[1]; iVar15=...+(bVar4&0x7f)*0x200 -> program=e[1]&0x7f); :32+:34+:41 (bVar1=pbVar9[2]; uVar12=bVar1>>4; iVar14=iVar15+uVar12*0x20 -> tone=e[2]>>4). FUN_80045024.c:50-54 case 3 confirms *(DAT_800ac778+0x14)=program-3/snd1 EDT. FUN_800396fc.c:12,22-28 proves +0x14 is a relocated offset INSIDE the loaded room sound block = rdt->snd_edt[1] (so the claim's 'it IS the snd1 EDT' is right; the PC comment audio_pc.c:402's 'separate overlay table 0x80123cd0' is WRONG). Our deviating hardcode: audio_psx.c:217-220 / audio_pc.c:482-485 em_resolve_pt(vab,0,ID+1). Byte-true reference path: vab_common.c:176-177 (program=e[1]&0x7f, tone=e[2]>>4) == FUN_800453d0 exactly.
verdict.fix_summary: REAL mechanism deviation: FUN_800453d0(ID) is fully data-driven (program=EDT[ID][1]&0x7f, tone=EDT[ID][2]>>4 from the room snd1/program-3 EDT at DAT_800ac778+0x14), but our code hardcodes program=0,tone=ID+1 and leaves s_em_edt=rdt->snd_edt[1] dead (assigned at audio_psx.c:168/202/214, never read). IMPORTANT CAVEAT (verified against the real extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1140/snd1.edt): for room1140 the EDT entries ARE exactly {prog0,tone=ID+1} for IDs 0-10 (ID0->t1...ID9->t10), so the hardcode is byte-IDENTICAL in effect for the only room with active zombies -> currently NO audible/byte difference in room1140. The deviation is latent/multi-room: ID17-21 in that same EDT use program 1, proving the table is genuinely data-driven, not a universal prog0/tone-N+1 pattern. Fix to make the mechanism byte-true: resolve via tn=NULL; vag=re15_se_resolve(rdt->snd_edt[1], &s_em_vab, ID, &tn) passing the RAW situation ID (detect=5, feed=3, bite=1, kick=7, got-hit=6, grab=9 -- NOTE bite=ID1 not the code-comment's ID2; the code passes tone2=ID1 which is correct, but several comments mislabel it), and drop the em_resolve_pt(0,ID+1) hardcode + the dead s_em_edt. re15_se_resolve already reads program=e[1]&0x7f, tone=e[2]>>4 and empty-slot e[2]==0&&e[3]==0 byte-true. Classify as deviation (mechanism), effort low; visually/audibly neutral for room1140.

claim: The feed/chew SE cadence is a synthesised modulo (timer % 69 == 0x28 with a per-slot phase offset i*23). The original fires the feed sound on the animation keyframe where the EDD emits the chomp marker character '(' (0x28) into entity[0x95], i.e. it is anim-data-driven, not a fixed 69-frame period. Our clip-length 69 and offset 23 are tunables, not read from the EDD.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801023d8.c:29-31 (if entity[0x95]=='(' -> func_0x800453d0(3)) and FUN_80103d48.c:25-26 (same) — feed SE is gated on the per-frame anim marker char entity[0x95]=='(' (0x28), set by the EDD keyframe, not a frame-count modulo
fix: Drive the chew SE off the actual feed clip's keyframe markers: detect the EDD/anim frame at which entity[0x95] would be '(' for clip 0x27 and fire then, instead of a hardcoded %69==0x28. Requires parsing the em10 bank1 EDD keyframe-event channel for clip 0x27. Until then, keep the synthesised cadence but mark it explicitly tunable (it already partly is).

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f8b4.c:76-80 (entity[0x95] = entity[0x95]+1; wraps to 0 when uVar9<=it => it is a per-clip FRAME INDEX, not a keyframe marker char); FUN_8001f8b4.c:24-27 (uVar9 = clip length read from anim table low16 at param_2+entity[0x94]*4 => length is DATA-DRIVEN, not a constant); FUN_801023d8.c:29 & FUN_80103d48.c:25 (`entity[0x95]=='('` i.e. frame index == 0x28 == 40 => fires on frame 40 of the feed clip). Our code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:196 ((ai->timer++ + i*23)%69==0x28).
verdict.fix_summary: A real deviation exists, but the audit's RATIONALE is wrong: entity[0x95] is NOT an EDD keyframe marker character — FUN_8001f8b4.c:76-80 proves it is the per-clip FRAME INDEX (a counter that increments each frame and wraps to 0 at the clip length uVar9). The '(' is just Ghidra rendering byte 0x28 = frame number 40. So the original is a frame-index check (`feed clip frame == 40`), exactly the kind of frame-count test the audit denied. The genuine byte-true gaps in our line are: (1) the wrap period 69 is a hardcoded TUNABLE — the original wraps entity[0x95] at the data-derived clip length read from the em10 bank1 anim table (FUN_8001f8b4.c:24-27, low16 of the entry at base+entity[0x94]*4), so we must read the real feed-clip (0x27) length instead of 69; (2) the `+ i*23` per-slot phase offset has NO disasm basis — each zombie's entity[0x95] is its own independent per-clip counter that naturally desyncs because feeders enter the FEED state at different times; the artificial stagger should be removed. Byte-true fix: track each AI zombie's own feed-clip frame index resetting to 0 on (re)entering FEED, increment per frame, wrap at the EDD clip length, and fire zb_play_se(-2) when that index == 40 (0x28) — no modulo-69, no i*23. The audit's suggested 'parse a keyframe-event channel' work is unnecessary.

loc: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:266-277 (ATTACK: bite SE fires at ai->timer >= ZB_LUNGE_HIT==32)
claim: The bite SE is fired at a fixed lunge frame (ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32, ~half of the 65-frame lunge). The original fires bite on the anim marker entity[0x95]=='#'||'P', a keyframe event in the lunge clip, not a fixed frame count. ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32 and ZB_LUNGE_FRAMES=65 are self-declared tunables (zombie_common.c:52-53).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:22-23 (bite SE gated on entity[0x95]=='#'||'P', set by the lunge clip's EDD keyframe)
fix: Fire the bite SE on the lunge clip's marker keyframe (the EDD event channel emitting '#'/'P'), not at frame 32. Parse clip 0x09's EDD event markers; until parsed, treat ZB_LUNGE_HIT as an explicit approximation.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102edc.c:22-23 — `if ((*(char*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x95)=='#')||(*(char*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x95)=='P')) func_0x800453d0(2);` gates the bite SE on the anim-marker byte entity[0x95], which is reset to 0 at clip-start (FUN_80102edc.c:12) and written by the EDD event channel during playback. Our code at psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:275-277 fires the bite at the fixed frame `ai->timer >= ZB_LUNGE_HIT`, and ZB_LUNGE_HIT==32 is a self-declared approximation (zombie_common.c:53 `/* bite/damage lands mid-grab (~half of 65f) */`). Sound ID matches (disasm func_0x800453d0(2) == our selector -3 → em_resolve_pt(...,0,2,...) at audio_psx.c:219), so only the TRIGGER TIMING deviates: marker-driven event vs hardcoded frame count.
verdict.fix_summary: Drive the bite SE off the EDD event-marker of attack clip 0x09 instead of frame 32: clear a per-zombie marker byte at clip-start (mirrors FUN_80102edc.c:12 entity[0x95]=0), advance the clip, and when the EDD event channel for clip 0x09 emits the '#'(0x23) or 'P'(0x50) keyframe (FUN_80102edc.c:22), fire zb_play_se(-3) once (and apply the grab there). Until the em10 bank-1 clip-0x09 EDD event channel is parsed, ZB_LUNGE_HIT=32 must stay labelled an explicit approximation, not byte-true. The disasm cite is real and refutation failed; this is a genuine timing-mechanism deviation, not an RE2-arch difference (nothing PC/SDL/synth-specific). I'd downgrade the claim's 'uncertain' to confirmed.

claim: The feed-chomp cadence uses an invented per-zombie phase stagger `+ (unsigned)i * 23u`. The original has NO such index-based offset: each feeding zombie sets its OWN per-clip frame index entity[0x95]=0 when it enters the feed clip and the chomp fires when THAT zombie's 0x95 reaches 0x28. The only de-sync between zombies in the original is the natural difference in when each one began feeding (different spawn/state-entry time), not a deterministic i*23 array-index term. The i*23 is a tuned heuristic to fake the savestate-observed stagger (frames 38/40/81) and is not byte-true.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80103d48.c:21 (`entity[0x95]=0` on feed entry) and :25-26 (`if(entity[0x95]=='(') func_0x800453d0(3)`); confirmed identical in FUN_801023d8.c:21,29-30 — no i*N phase term anywhere
fix: Drive the chomp off the zombie's REAL per-clip frame index (entity[0x95] analogue = re15_actor_t.anim_frame, mapped at re15_actor.h:82/171). Replace `if (((ai->timer++ + (unsigned)i*23u) % 69u) == 0x28u) zb_play_se(cam,z,-2);` with `if (z->anim_frame == 0x28) zb_play_se(cam,z,-2);` (in the feed clip). The per-zombie stagger then emerges naturally from each zombie's own anim_frame phase, exactly as in the original.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80103d48.c:21 (`*(undefined1*)(+0x95)=0` on feed-entry) and :25-26 (`if(+0x95=='(') func_0x800453d0(3)`), identical in FUN_801023d8.c:21,29-30 — chomp fires when THAT zombie's own per-clip frame index 0x95 == 0x28, no i*N phase term. Our anim_frame IS the byte-true 0x95 analogue: re15_actor.h:178,183-185 (`re15_actor_set_motion` resets anim_frame=0 on motion change, comment: "original engine clears actor+0x06/0x07/0x95") and player_common.c:344 increments it per tick. Thus the deviating line zombie_common.c:196 (`((ai->timer++ + (unsigned)i*23u) % 69u) == 0x28`) is an invented array-index heuristic; its premise comment at :192 ("our enemy anim_frame is a FREE-RUNNING uint8 (no per-clip reset)") is factually wrong — anim_frame DOES reset per clip (re15_actor.h:183-185) and zombie_common.c:189 calls set_motion(ZB_CLIP_FEED) which performs that reset.
verdict.fix_summary: Drive the chomp off the zombie's own per-clip frame index instead of a fabricated i*23 stagger. The 0x95 comparison in the original is a 30Hz clip-frame index, so it is byte-true to compare the clip-frame index to 0x28. Replace zombie_common.c:196 `if(((ai->timer++ + (unsigned)i*23u)%69u)==0x28u) zb_play_se(...)` with a frame-index test. On PSX anim_frame advances at 30Hz (==0x95 directly): `if(z->anim_frame==0x28) zb_play_se(cam,z,-2);`. For PC (anim_frame runs at 60fps, see game_step_common.c:93 "PC halves anim_frame at 60fps") use the clip-frame index `(z->anim_frame>>1)` and edge-gate it so the chomp fires exactly once per cycle (avoid a bare == that a 60fps counter could skip). The inter-zombie stagger (savestate frames 38/40/81) then emerges naturally from each zombie's independent feed-entry tick (each resets its own anim_frame to 0 via set_motion(ZB_CLIP_FEED) at zombie_common.c:189), not from a deterministic array-index term. Also fix the false comment at zombie_common.c:192. Verify the feed clip is exactly 69 frames so the index reaches 0x28 each loop.

claim: The cadence period is hardcoded to `% 69u` (a magic number; comment claims feed clip 0x27 = 69 frames). The original period is the ACTUAL feed-clip length, applied by the engine's frame-wrap (entity[0x95] increments +1/frame and wraps to 0 when it reaches the clip-length parameter). 69 is an asserted constant, not a cited byte offset; if the real em10/16 clip-0x27 frame count differs, the chomp drifts. ai->timer is also a SEPARATE free-running counter, decoupled from the actual animation playback rate / freeze logic, so a paused/frozen feed clip still advances the SE timer.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f3bc.c:89-93 — `bVar3 = entity[0x95]+1; entity[0x95]=bVar3; if (param_4 <= bVar3) entity[0x95]=0;` (frame index advances and wraps at the clip-length param, not at a literal 69)
fix: Use z->anim_frame (which is advanced and wrapped by the same keyframe code as the original 0x95) so the period is the real clip length and the SE only fires when the animation actually reaches frame 0x28. This drops the literal 69 and the parallel free-running ai->timer entirely (the timer field can stay for other states).

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f3bc.c:89-93 (entity[0x95]=entity[0x95]+1; wraps to 0 when param_4<=it) + RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80103d48.c:25 (`if (*(char*)(_DAT_800ac784+0x95)=='(') func_0x800453d0(3);` — feed SE fires off the SAME 0x95 anim-frame counter at value 0x28). Our deviating impl: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:196 uses a SEPARATE free-running `(ai->timer++ + i*23u) % 69u == 0x28u`. The engine already has the real 0x95 equivalent: anim_frame advanced at player_common.c:344 and wrapped at the real clip->frame_count at anim_select_common.c:46.
verdict.fix_summary: Confirmed deviation in MECHANISM. The original fires the feed/chomp SE off the entity's REAL animation frame counter entity[0x95] reaching exactly 0x28 (FUN_80103d48.c:25), where 0x95 is the per-clip frame index advanced +1/frame and wrapped to 0 at the actual clip-length param (FUN_8001f3bc.c:89-93). Our code instead runs a PARALLEL free-running `ai->timer` with a hardcoded `% 69u` modulus and a synthetic `i*23` phase offset — structurally decoupled from playback. The engine already maintains the correct counter: anim_frame (advanced player_common.c:344, wrapped at clip->frame_count anim_select_common.c:46), which IS the byte-true entity[0x95]. Byte-true fix: in RE15_ZB_FEED, edge-detect `(z->anim_frame % feed_clip_frame_count) == 0x28` (frame_count read from the loaded em10 bank-1 EDD clip 0x27, NOT the literal 69) and drop both the parallel ai->timer and the invented i*23 phase — the natural per-entity stagger then comes from each zombie's own anim_frame reset (FUN_80103d48.c:21 zeroes 0x95 on feed entry). Caveats softening severity: (1) the literal 69 is asserted byte-true from EDD @0x3da8 (zombie_common.c:71), and if our extracted clip 0x27 frame_count==69 the audible PERIOD is already correct today, so this is primarily a brittleness/decoupling fix not a wrong-pitch-of-chomp bug; (2) the 'frozen clip still ticks SE' concern does not manifest in FEED specifically since anim_active=0 means the feed clip is not frozen. Still a real mechanism deviation worth fixing per the cite-the-real-counter rule.

claim: Pursue forward speed is WRONG and the comment misattributes the byte-true value. Our code translates ZB_STEP=0x200 per frame and the comment claims '0x474 at FUN_80102bc8.c:20 is the anim/Y-arm value, NOT the XZ step'. That is provably false: the original WALK speed is entity[0x8c]=0x474, and FUN_800245d8 reads entity[0x8c] as the move velocity, rotates it by RotMatrixY(entity[0x6a]) and adds the result to entity[0x34]/[0x3c] (the X/Z world position). So 0x474 IS the forward XZ step magnitude; 0x200 is unrelated (it is the SE-event-code arg a3 passed to the anim routine func_0x8001f314, see FUN_80102bc8.c:31-32). Our walk is ~2.25x too slow and cites the wrong constant.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:20 (entity[0x8c]=0x474) and ghidra1_V2.txt:106718 (FUN_800245d8: lhu v0,0x8c(v0) -> SVECTOR.vz) + ghidra1_V2.txt:106746,106769-106778 (RotMatrixY(entity[0x6a]) then entity[0x34]+=rx, entity[0x3c]+=rz)
fix: Set the pursue forward step to entity[0x8c]=0x474 (1140), i.e. ZB_STEP=0x474, applied along rot_y exactly as zb_step_forward already does. Fix the comment: 0x474 is the XZ velocity (entity[0x8c]) consumed by FUN_800245d8; 0x200 is the anim SE-event arg, not a translation step. (If avg apparent speed must drop because the original only moves on the anim keyframe event per FUN_80102bc8.c:33, gate the 0x474 step on the clip's step keyframe rather than scaling the constant down.)

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:106718 (lhu v0,0x8c(v0)) -> ghidra1_V2.txt:106722 (sh v0,local_30 = SVECTOR.vz) -> ghidra1_V2.txt:106744-106746 (RotMatrixY(entity[0x6a])) -> ghidra1_V2.txt:106769-106778 (rotated X added to entity[0x34], rotated Z added to entity[0x3c]). entity[0x8c]=0x474 set in RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:20. The 0x200 is the a3 arg to func_0x8001f314 (FUN_80102bc8.c:31-32), and FUN_8001f314 is an ANIM routine (ghidra1_V2.txt:98298 lbu v0,0x94(t0) = clip index; 98316-98333 dispatch to anim-frame advance FUN_8001f3bc/FUN_8001f8b4) that never writes entity[0x34]/[0x3c]. ATTACK path FUN_80102edc.c:9 sets the SAME field entity[0x8c]=0x1e and our code (zombie_common.c:269) correctly treats THAT as the XZ step — proving our PURSUE comment (zombie_common.c:39-42) is internally inconsistent and backwards.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation, confirmed against disasm. The pursue forward XZ step is entity[0x8c]=0x474 (consumed by FUN_800245d8 = rotate SVECTOR.vz=0x474 by yaw[0x6a], add to pos[0x34]/[0x3c]), NOT 0x200. 0x200 is the a3 anim/SE arg to FUN_8001f314 (an animation-advance routine keyed on entity[0x94], no position write). Our ZB_STEP=0x200 makes the walk ~2.25x too slow and the zombie_common.c:39-42 comment misattributes both constants. Fix: rename/define the pursue XZ step to 0x474 (entity[0x8c]), apply it along rot_y exactly as zb_step_forward already does, and correct the comment (0x474=XZ velocity entity[0x8c] consumed by FUN_800245d8; 0x200=anim arg to FUN_8001f314). Caveat (already flagged by the audit): in the original the 0x474 step fires only when FUN_8001f314 returns nonzero (FUN_80102bc8.c:33 iVar3!=0), i.e. on the walk-clip's move keyframe, not every frame. To stay byte-true the engine must gate the 0x474 step on that keyframe event rather than translating 0x474 every frame (which would be too fast); do NOT just bump 0x200->0x474 unconditionally without the keyframe gate, or average speed will be wrong.

claim: The 'only-advance-when-facing' dot>0 gate is a heuristic with no counterpart in the original pursue handler. FUN_80102bc8 (walk row) calls the mover FUN_800245d8(0) unconditionally once the anim-step event fires (FUN_80102bc8.c:33->40); it moves the body forward along the CURRENT facing entity[0x6a] every step regardless of whether the player is already in the forward hemisphere. The zombie only TURNS toward the player at rate 0x10/frame (func_0x8001aa68(...,0x10)); it never stops translating because the player slipped behind it. Our dot>0 stop is an invented anti-drift hack.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:33-40 (move on anim event, no facing test) + FUN_80102bc8.c:54 (turn toward stored target angle at rate 0x10)
fix: Remove the dot>0 advance gate from PURSUE (and STANDUP). Always step forward along rot_y at the byte-true speed; rely solely on the 0x10/frame turn (zb_turn_toward) to re-aim. The body naturally curves toward the player as the original does.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:33-40 (anim-step gate at :33 -> mover func_0x800245d8(0) called UNCONDITIONALLY at :40, no facing test) and FUN_80102bc8.c:54 (re-aim only, turn toward stored angle at rate 0x10). Mover RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800245d8.c:33,45-46 confirms it RotMatrixY's entity[0x6a]=current facing and adds the forward vector to x(0x34)/z(0x3c) every call. Our hack: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:248 (PURSUE 'step>0 && dot>0') and :222 (STANDUP 'dist>GRAB && dot>0'); the comment :245-247 admits it exists to suppress 'post-bite drift', not as a port.
verdict.fix_summary: Remove the '&& dot > 0' advance gate from PURSUE (zombie_common.c:248) and the 'dot > 0' from STANDUP (:222). Always step forward along rot_y via zb_step_forward (the byte-true FUN_800245d8 mover) once the GRAB-ring cap allows, and rely solely on zb_turn_toward(...,0x10) to re-aim each frame, exactly as the original (mover unconditional on anim event, turn at 0x10/frame). The body then naturally curves toward the player. Keep the GRAB_DIST step cap (prevents passing through the player) and the existing turn; only the dot gate is invented. Note separately: the byte-true walk speed is entity[0x8c]=0x474 (FUN_80102bc8.c:20 / FUN_800245d8.c:17), whereas ZB_STEP=0x200 is used here — that is a distinct speed-value question outside this finding.

claim: The 'never translate past ZB_GRAB_DIST=350' step cap is an invented constant. No 350-unit stop ring exists in the original pursue/lunge handlers. The original pursue (FUN_80102bc8) does not cap the forward step by remaining distance at all; the pursue->attack handoff is governed by the FSM in FUN_80101364 (entity[0x1d0] distance < 2000 AND cone) and by the wall/contact flag in FUN_80102df8 (entity[0x1c4]&0x2000), not by a step clamp. 350 has no disasm origin (it is described in our own comments as preventing 'fly-through-the-player oscillation' — an admitted heuristic).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:31-41 (mover called with no distance cap) + FUN_80101364.c:20 (attack handoff = dist<2000) + FUN_80102df8.c:16-18 (contact via entity[0x1c4]&0x2000)
fix: Drop the ZB_GRAB_DIST step cap from the movement path. Stop pursuing by transitioning to ATTACK when the byte-true conditions are met (linear dist entity[0x1d0] < 2000 AND inside the ±0x2c8 cone), and let the attack/contact logic handle the final closing — do not clamp the per-frame translation by an invented 350u ring.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102bc8.c:31-33 (pursue mover func_0x8001f314(x,z,0,0x200) — fixed step, no dist clamp; contact handled by if(iVar3==0)return) + FUN_80102edc.c:20-21 (lunge mover func_0x8001f314(...,0x100) — fixed step) + FUN_80101364.c:20 (attack handoff = entity[0x1d0]<2000 AND cone) + FUN_80102df8.c:16-18 (contact/grab via entity[0x1c4]&0x2000). The literal 350/0x15e appears NOWHERE in the STAGE1 overlay (grep: no matches); every distance gate uses entity[0x1d0] thresholds (0x4b0/2000/0x5dc/3000/4000) for state transitions only, never to clamp the per-frame translation.
verdict.fix_summary: Remove the ZB_GRAB_DIST=350 step cap from the movement path in zombie_common.c (the `step = min(step, dist - ZB_GRAB_DIST)` clamps at lines 224, 244, 271 and the `dist > ZB_GRAB_DIST` gates at 222, 268). Byte-true: pursue/lunge translate a FIXED step (0x200 pursue per FUN_80102bc8:31-32, 0x1e/0x100 lunge per FUN_80102edc:9,20) through the collision-constrained mover (re15_collision_constrain already plays the role of func_0x8001f314's blocking `if(iVar3==0)return`). Drive the pursue→attack handoff purely on the FSM test entity[0x1d0]<2000 AND inside the ±0x2c8 cone (FUN_80101364:20), and let the contact flag entity[0x1c4]&0x2000 (FUN_80102df8:16) — i.e. wall/player collision — govern the final stop, rather than an invented 350u ring. Note the original's "never pass through the player" emerges from collision + the contact-flag transition, so verify re15_collision_constrain treats the player as a blocker (or add the player-AABB contact test) when dropping the ring, otherwise the lunge could visibly overshoot.

claim: The pursue->attack trigger is missing the byte-true facing-cone requirement. We enter ATTACK purely on dist<=ZB_LUNGE_DIST(2000). The original requires BOTH entity[0x1d0] < 2000 AND the ±0x2c8 cone test func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x2c8)!=0 before setting the attack state word entity[4]=0x701. Without the cone, our zombie can launch an attack while the player is off to the side/behind.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80101364.c:20-22 (if (entity[0x1d0] < 2000 && cone(0x2c8)!=0) entity[4]=0x701)
fix: Gate PURSUE->ATTACK on dist<2000 AND the ±0x2c8 facing cone. We already compute fwd-dot for the cone; reuse it with the 0x2c8 (62.6deg) half-angle threshold (dot>0 && dot*dot > (cos(0x2c8)*4096)^2 * d2) exactly mirroring the existing ZB_COS_CONE2 pattern but with the 0x2c8 cosine.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80101364.c:13,20-22 — sVar2=func_0x8001a9cc(&DAT_800aca88,0x2c8); if((entity[0x1d0]<2000)&&(sVar2!=0)) entity[4]=0x701. func_0x8001a9cc (RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001a9cc.c:8-16) is a facing-cone test: it returns 0 unless the entity→player relative angle is within ±param2, so 0x2c8 = ±62.6°. State 0x701 is the attack state executed by FUN_80102edc.c:9-11 (entity[0x8c]=0x1e lunge speed, clip 0x94=0x1a). Our code psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:238-251 enters RE15_ZB_ATTACK in the `else` (dist<=ZB_LUNGE_DIST 2000) with NO facing gate; the `faced` cone (line 180) is used only for IDLE→PURSUE (line 209), never for PURSUE→ATTACK.
verdict.fix_summary: Gate PURSUE→ATTACK (zombie_common.c:249) on dist<2000 AND the ±0x2c8 facing cone, mirroring FUN_80101364.c:20-22. Add a new cosine constant for 0x2c8 (62.6°): ZB_COS_CONE2_ATTACK = (cos(0x2c8/4096*360°)*4096)² = (0.4592*4096)² ≈ 3,537,000 (NOT the existing ZB_COS_CONE2=2563201, which is the 0x5f4/±67° aggro cone). Reuse the already-computed dot/d2: faced_attack = (dot>0) && (dot*dot > ZB_COS_CONE2_ATTACK * d2). In the `else` branch, only transition to ATTACK when dist<2000 && faced_attack; otherwise keep shambling/turning. NB the original uses func_0x8001a9cc's exact integer angle math (entity→target atan2 minus entity facing at +0x6a, threshold ±0x2c8) rather than a cosine dot — for a fully byte-true port consider replicating that signed-angle comparison instead of the float-cosine approximation.

claim: Our mover wall-constrains every step via re15_collision_constrain. The original FUN_800245d8 does NOT clamp position against walls; it adds the raw rotated velocity to entity[0x34]/[0x3c]. Wall handling in the original is via separate contact flags (entity[0x1c4]&0x2000) feeding the FSM, not an in-mover constrain. This is likely an acceptable engine-port necessity but is not byte-identical to the original mover.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:106769-106778 (FUN_800245d8 adds rotated velocity to position with no collision clamp) + FUN_80102df8.c:16-18 (contact handled separately via entity[0x1c4]&0x2000)
fix: Verify whether the per-step collision constrain changes the path vs the original (which lets the body push into geometry until the contact flag flips state). If it diverges visibly, move wall handling to a contact-flag->FSM transition mirroring FUN_80102df8 rather than clamping the mover; otherwise document it as an engine-port choice.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:106702-106777 (FUN_800245d8 body: lh yaw 0x6a -> RotMatrixY -> ldv0/RTV0 GTE rotate -> addu v0,v0,v1; sw v0,0x34(a0) @106693-106697/0x800246e0-e4 and sw v0,0x3c(a0) @0x800246f4-f8 -- raw rotated-velocity add to entity[0x34]/[0x3c], NO collision/clamp call anywhere in the function); cite 106769-106778 is genuine and is exactly that position-add block. RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_80102df8.c:16-18 (original wall/contact handled via entity[0x1c4]&0x2000 -> FSM state write entity[4]=0x601, NOT a mover clamp). Our deviation: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/zombie_common.c:136 calls re15_collision_constrain in zb_step_forward; and re15_collision.c:177/180 + the only callers (room_common.c:129, aot_common.c:448, stair_common.c:126, game_step_common.c:75) prove s_coll_band is derived ONLY from the PLAYER's Y -- so a zombie is push-out tested against the player's-band SCA wall cells, a behavior the byte-true mover FUN_800245d8 does not have.
verdict.fix_summary: Real (citable) byte-true deviation, low severity. The byte-true zombie mover FUN_800245d8 adds the raw rotated velocity to entity[0x34]/[0x3c] with no wall clamp; wall handling is a SEPARATE contact-flag mechanism (entity[0x1c4]&0x2000 -> FSM state write, FUN_80102df8). Our zb_step_forward instead clamps via re15_collision_constrain, which is the PLAYER resolver band-gated on the player's s_coll_band (never re-derived per zombie) -- so it constrains zombies against the player's-band cells, which the original mover never does. Byte-true fix: drop re15_collision_constrain from zb_step_forward (apply raw nx/nz like FUN_800245d8), and if walls matter for the FSM, port the contact-flag path (set a per-zombie contact bit on penetration and feed it to the FSM as a state transition mirroring FUN_80102df8 entity[4]=0x601), rather than clamping the mover. Not engine_specific (not in the accepted SDL/synth/input category); it is a port shortcut. Low priority for room1140 (open-floor feeding; homing already capped by the separate ZB_GRAB_DIST ring so the visible path barely changes), so the audit's 'uncertain' is understated but the practical impact is minor.

claim: The periodic grab-bite is a synthesized mechanic with no disasm basis: PLY_GRAB_BITE_PERIOD=40 (a bite every 40 frames) and PLY_GRAB_BITE_DMG=10 are applied by hand inside our PLY_GRAB phase loop. The original has NO 'grab-bite-every-N-frames' construct. In the EXE all player damage flows through FUN_80012d60, which subtracts DMG_TABLE[type] ONCE per accepted enemy-hit collision (FUN_80012d60:38 'DAT_800acaee - *(short*)(&DAT_8006f418+type*2)') gated by the i-frame latch DAT_800acae7&1 (FUN_80012d60:37) and re-armed by the attacker, not by a fixed 40-frame timer in the victim. The 40-frame period is invented.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80012d60.c:37-38
fix: Remove the self-timed bite. Drive grab damage the byte-true way: the zombie (overlay) attack handler calls the damage entry FUN_80012d60 with the attack type; damage is DMG_TABLE[type] applied once per accepted collision while DAT_800acae7&1==0. Port that: have the zombie grab handler invoke re15_player_damage(type, gx, gz) each bite, and let the existing i-frame gate (s_iframe / DAT_800acae7&1) throttle it, rather than a hardcoded 40-frame counter. DMG value itself must come from DMG_TABLE[type] (table is correct), not a literal 10 in the player FSM.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_Overlays/STAGE1/FUN_801035e8.c:19-20 (range-gated `_DAT_800acaee = _DAT_800acaee + -1;` = HP-1-per-frame eat drain, the byte-true grab/eat mechanism, called every frame from FUN_80102bc8.c:24,44,60); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80012d60.c:37-38,51 (discrete hits subtract DMG_TABLE[type] once, gated by `(DAT_800acae7 & 1)==0` i-frame latch then `|= 1`); no 40-frame/10-dmg construct exists anywhere.
verdict.fix_summary: REFUTATION FAILED — the deviation is REAL. Our PLY_GRAB_BITE_PERIOD=40 / PLY_GRAB_BITE_DMG=10 (player_damage_common.c:48-49 applied at :164-167) is invented and matches no disasm. CONFIRMED: the original has no victim-side periodic-bite timer. For discrete enemy hits, FUN_80012d60.c:38 subtracts DMG_TABLE[type] exactly ONCE per accepted collision, gated by the i-frame latch DAT_800acae7&1 (set at :51, cleared at clip-end @0x80031964) — re-armed by the react clip, not a fixed timer. The CLAIM's amount/cadence is right that ours is fabricated, but its proposed fix (route grab damage through FUN_80012d60+DMG_TABLE) is itself slightly off: the actual GRAB/EAT drain is NOT via FUN_80012d60. It is in the STAGE1 zombie overlay handler FUN_801035e8 (the eat/grab state, called every frame from FUN_80102bc8): it decrements player HP by 1 (FUN_801035e8.c:20) ONLY when the mouth-proximity test func_0x8001f314(...,0x200) returns nonzero (line 19) — i.e. HP minus-1-per-frame while the zombie's mouth is within 0x200 of Leon, attacker-driven. BYTE-TRUE FIX: drive the grab drain from the zombie overlay port (re15_zombie/zombie_common), decrementing player HP by 1 each frame the grab is active AND the mouth is within 0x200 units of the player, replacing the 40-frame/10-dmg block in player_damage_common.c:164-167. Keep discrete bites on the existing FUN_80012d60 path (DMG_TABLE[type], i-frame gated). Confidence high on the deviation; the precise fix should cite FUN_801035e8.c:19-20 rather than the claim's FUN_80012d60 route.

claim: The entire struggle/mash-to-escape + kick-off mechanic (PLY_GRAB_STRUGGLE_MAX=100, PLY_GRAB_STRUGGLE_DECAY=1, re15_player_grab_struggle(), the s_grab_struggle>=MAX -> s_kicked=1 kick-off branch, PLY_KICK_FRAMES=14, PLY_KICK_STEP=45) is invented. The focus claims FUN_80011f50 holds a 'struggle/kick flag', but FUN_80011f50 is the ENEMY hit-target selection routine (param_1 = attack type 1/7/8; it walks the enemy list DAT_800acc2c, scores candidates, sets entity+0x93 hit bits and entity+5 = attack type) - it contains NO player-side mash counter, escape threshold, or kick. There is no cited mash/kick FSM anywhere in the player damage path.
fix: Do not ship the mash/kick mechanic as byte-true. The real grab/eaten progression runs through the player major-state handlers state5=LAB_80036834 and state6=LAB_800368c0 (dispatch table PTR_LAB_80073f90[5]/[6], ghidra1_V2.txt:242025-242026), which each call a SUB-handler via DAT_800acbfc[8] indexing tables DAT_800ac758 (state5) / DAT_800ac858 (state6) gated on the substate byte DAT_800aca5a. RE those sub-handler tables to recover the actual grab->eaten->death progression and whatever pad-driven escape (if any) exists, then port that. Until cited, mark the kick/mash block as uncertain/placeholder, not byte-true.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80011f50.c:42-67,144-181 (enemy hit-target selection: param_1=attack type, walks enemy list &DAT_800acc2c stride 0x7d, sets entity+0x93 hit bits and entity+5=param_1 — NO player mash/kick counter); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80012d60.c:47-56 (player damage entry sets state DAT_800aca58=2 HIT / =3 DEATH only — no grab/mash/kick); ghidra1_V2.txt:242025-242026 (PTR_LAB_80073f90[5]=LAB_80036834,[6]=LAB_800368c0); ghidra1_V2.txt:132798-132871 (state5/state6 handlers read substate DAT_800aca5a then dispatch via DAT_800acbfc[8]-indexed sub-tables DAT_800ac758/DAT_800ac858 — a timed substate FSM); ghidra1_V2.txt:473567,473946 (those sub-tables are runtime-filled, all-zero in static export = static dead-end). Our invented mash/kick: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/player_damage_common.c:50-53,80-86,170-179.
verdict.fix_summary: The mash-to-escape + kick-off mechanic (PLY_GRAB_STRUGGLE_MAX=100, PLY_GRAB_STRUGGLE_DECAY, re15_player_grab_struggle(), the s_grab_struggle>=MAX -> s_kicked kick-off branch at lines 170-179, PLY_KICK_FRAMES/STEP) has NO disasm basis and the file's own comments (lines 43-54) admit it is 'tuned'/not in the savestates = invented. FUN_80011f50 is enemy target-selection, not a player FSM, so the original focus mis-cited it. The real grab/eaten progression is the player major-state 5/6 handlers LAB_80036834/LAB_800368c0, which run a deterministic substate FSM gated on DAT_800aca5a via runtime sub-handler tables DAT_800ac758/DAT_800ac858 — no pad-driven escape is visible, and the 6 room1140 savestates show a straight hp100->-1 grab->eaten->death with no escape. Byte-true fix: drop the mash/kick block; model grab as the substate-timed grab->eaten->death progression (states 5->6->7). Since DAT_800ac758/858 are runtime-filled (static dead-end), recover the sub-handler timings via a DuckStation RAM trace of DAT_800aca5a across savestate_1..6 before re-implementing. Until then mark the kick/mash as placeholder, not byte-true.

claim: Internal contradiction / unproven HIT clip: the comment at lines 110-116 asserts 'BYTE-TRUE hit-react clip = 0x0a for ALL damage', but PLY_CLIP_HIT is #defined to 0x02 (line 29) and that is what re15_actor_set_motion plays at line 140. Neither 0x0a nor 0x02 is a cited body-clip index - FUN_80012d60 never writes a literal clip; it writes substate DAT_800aca59=dir+2 and calls the effect-sprite FUN_800453d0(10). The '0x0a' in the comment conflates the effect-sprite arg (param_1=10) with a body clip.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80012d60.c:40
fix: Resolve the comment-vs-define mismatch and cite the real body clip. The body react in state 2 comes from the state-2 dispatcher (LAB_80035af0, PTR_LAB_80073f90[2]) using substate DAT_800aca59 = FUN_8001a7a8(dir)+2; RE LAB_80035af0's substate sub-handlers to get the actual entity[0x94] write, then set PLY_CLIP_HIT from that. Do not label 0x0a as byte-true.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:131929-131931 (LAB_80035bbc, HIT ROW substate-2 handler FUN_80035b70: `ori v0,zero,0xa` then `sb v0,-0x3518(at)=>DAT_800acae8`) and ghidra1_V2.txt:132014-132016 (LAB_80035cf4, substate-3 handler FUN_80035ca8: identical `ori v0,zero,0xa`; `sb v0,...DAT_800acae8`). DAT_800acae8 = entity[0x94] (DAT_800ac784 base = &DAT_800aca54 = 0x800aca54; +0x94 = 0x800acae8). FUN_8001f314.c:8 consumes `*(byte*)(DAT_800ac784+0x94)` as the clip index into the player anim bank. So the HIT body clip is byte-true 0x0a in BOTH directions. Our deviation: player_damage_common.c:29 `#define PLY_CLIP_HIT 0x02`, used at line 116/140, so the damage-HIT path plays clip 0x02, not 0x0a.
verdict.fix_summary: The audit's premise is REFUTED: 0x0a IS a cited byte-true body clip, not a conflation with the effect-sprite arg. FUN_80012d60.c:40 `FUN_800453d0(10)` is indeed only the hit effect-sprite/particle spawner (FUN_800453d0.c writes to the DAT_800b242x particle table, param<0x19), and FUN_80012d60 itself only writes substate DAT_800aca59=dir+2 — but the BODY clip is set downstream: the state-2 dispatcher LAB_80035af0 jumps via PTR_LAB_800741a8[substate] into FUN_80035b70 (substate2) / FUN_80035ca8 (substate3), and BOTH unconditionally write literal 0xa to entity[0x94] (DAT_800acae8), which FUN_8001f314 uses as the clip index. So 0x0a is the proven HIT clip in both directions. The REAL deviation (different from the audit's framing): our `#define PLY_CLIP_HIT 0x02` (player_damage_common.c:29) is wrong for the damage-HIT path — it plays the savestate grab/struggle clip 0x02 instead of the disasm-proven 0x0a. Fix: set the HIT-path clip to 0x0a (the comment at 110-116 is already correct; the define/usage contradicts it). Note the GRAB path (state 5, clips 0x01/0x02/0x06) is a separate savestate-derived mechanism and is unaffected — only the damage-HIT (state PLY_HIT) react clip should be 0x0a.

claim: Duration/knockback magic numbers are explicitly tuned, not cited: PLY_HIT_FRAMES=24, PLY_DEATH_FRAMES=44, PLY_KB_FRAMES=6, PLY_KB_STEP=33 (the comment claims '~200 total = byte-true 0xc8' but 0xc8=200 is asserted, not cited), PLY_KICK_FRAMES=14, PLY_KICK_STEP=45, PLY_EATEN_FRAMES=50. The original ends these states on the clip's own keyframe wrap, not on fixed frame counts (comment lines 33-34 admit this).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80012d60.c:51
fix: Replace fixed frame counts with the clip's real keyframe length (from the EDD/EMR anim tables of the played clip) and terminate the state on clip wrap, matching the EXE which clears the i-frame latch (DAT_800acae7|1 set at FUN_80012d60:51) when the react clip ends. The 0xc8 knockback total needs a real cite from the state-2 knockback sub-handler (LAB_80035b70/LAB_80035ca8) before it can be called byte-true.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:131958-131962 (HIT sub-handler LAB_80035b70 advances DAT_800aca5a += FUN_8001f314() clip-player return, a3=0x200 — clip-keyframe driven, no fixed count); ghidra1_V2.txt:131969-131971 (sub-phase 2 clears i-frame latch via andi 0xfe on completion); ghidra1_V2.txt:132394-132414 (DEATH switchD_8003611c case-3: DAT_800aca5a += FUN_8001f314() AND DAT_800acae0 -= 0xf/frame countdown — not 24/44/50); ghidra1_V2.txt:132422/132503/132690 (latch cleared 'sb zero' on state end); ghidra1_V2.txt:131929-131931 (knockback magnitude DAT_800acae8 = 0xa, NOT 0xc8/200); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80012d60.c:51 (cite real: DAT_800acae7 |= 1 sets the i-frame latch on hit); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8001f314.c:8 (FUN_8001f314 is the clip player keyed on motion id DAT_800ac784+0x94)
verdict.fix_summary: Confirmed deviation — could not refute. Our player_damage_common.c terminates HIT/DEATH/EATEN/KB/KICK on fixed frame counts (PLY_HIT_FRAMES=24, PLY_DEATH_FRAMES=44, PLY_EATEN_FRAMES=50, PLY_KB_FRAMES=6, PLY_KB_STEP=33, PLY_KICK_FRAMES=14/STEP=45), all admitted-tuned in our own comments (lines 33-34,44,52-54). The EXE instead runs a sub-phase state machine on DAT_800aca5a advanced by the clip player FUN_8001f314's return (clip-keyframe/wrap signal, a3=0x200), with a DAT_800acae0 -= 0xf/frame countdown gating the death sequence, and clears the i-frame latch (DAT_800acae7 &= 0xfe) when the react clip completes (LAB_80035c5c/LAB_80035d94, switchD_8003611c case-4 sb zero @132422). The cite FUN_80012d60.c:51 is real (latch SET on hit). The '≈200 total = byte-true 0xc8' comment is fabricated: the real knockback magnitude is DAT_800acae8=0xa(10) (ghidra1_V2.txt:131929), there is no 0xc8/200 constant in the handler. Byte-true fix: drive state termination off the played clip's real EDD/EMR keyframe count (terminate on clip wrap / FUN_8001f314 progress) and decrement DAT_800acae0 by 0xf/frame for the death sequence, instead of hardcoded counts; set knockback magnitude to 10 (0xa) per the i-frame handler. The prior audit's caveat stands: the exact knockback step needs the FUN_80045630 collapse-physics sub-handler (uses DAT_800acae8=0xa) confirmed before calling the per-frame step byte-true.

claim: DMG_TABLE declared as int16_t[16] with last 4 entries {771,2569,3595,4111}. The real damage table @0x8006f418 is only 12 s16 entries (0x418..0x42f). Bytes decode EXACTLY to {10,20,1000,1000,1000,50,100,200,300,1000,0,0}; our entries 12-15 are an over-read into the unrelated DAT_8006f430 byte-array (react-clip selector 03 03 09 0a 0b...). 771=0x0303 and 2569=0x0a09 are literally DAT_8006f430 bytes reinterpreted as s16. Any type 12-15 reads garbage damage.
