Our deviation: scd_vm.c:336 (scd_vm_init memset->0) and scd_room_setup.c:112 (scd_room_reenter memset->0) leave work_vars[0..3]=0, never the -1 sentinel. CONCRETE OBSERVABLE DIVERGENCE: extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1190/scd/sub01.c (and room1191) gate a jump-table on var0: `Cmp(0 >= 0)`/`Cmp(0 <= 4)` then `switch(0){case 0: ... Evt_exec(10,0x1802)}`. The first operand of Cmp is the VAR INDEX (SCDScriptDisassembler.java:3001 formatByteHex(var)), so this clamps work_vars[0] to [0,4]. Original var0=-1 => `>=0` false => switch skipped on entry; our var0=0 => case 0 fires a spurious Evt_exec. Real byte-true deviation.
verdict.fix_summary: After the memset in BOTH scd_vm_init (scd_vm.c:336) and scd_room_reenter (scd_room_setup.c:112), set g_scd.work_vars[0]=work_vars[1]=work_vars[2]=work_vars[3]=(int16_t)-1 (0xFFFF) to mirror FUN_8003ebf4, which runs on every room load (and at boot). Leave work_vars[10] (the scenario) untouched — the original preserves it (FUN_800396fc.c:53) and scd_room_setup.c:154 already re-stamps it. The original also clears DAT_800b102c=0 (a separate var) which our zero-memset already covers, so no extra work there. Verified the practical-impact case: rooms 1190/1191 sub01 switch(0) with a -1-guarded case 0; with the fix the spurious entry-frame Evt_exec(10,0x1802) is suppressed exactly as in the original.

claim: Se_on (SCD 0x36) is played LINEAR (tone.vol·0x3FFF/127, no square law). The original routes EVERY keyed SE voice through SpuVmKeyOnNow, which squares the magnitude: SPU_CH_VOL = mag²/0x3fff. So even non-positional Se_on (which uses the tone's default attenuation, not distance) is still subject to the square law. Our linear Se_on therefore plays markedly louder than the original. The code comment 'LINEAR (user-validated)' is a tuned exception, not byte-true.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/SpuVmKeyOnNow.c:47-48  ((&DAT_8008f6ac)[uVar6*8] = (short)((uVar8*uVar8)/0x3fff); (&DAT_8008f6ae)[uVar6*8] = (short)((uVar2*uVar2)/0x3fff);)  — reached for the Se_on path via FUN_8002c444.c:20/99 FUN_80045024(0x20.....,pos) and FUN_8004a0cc.c:106/113/119 FUN_80045024(0x40...,0)
fix: Route Se_on through the same square-law cascade as footsteps: in play_room_se compute spu = (tone.vol·0x3FFF/127)²/0x3FFF (i.e. call se_vol_byte_true with att=0x7f), instead of the flat tone.vol·0x3FFF/127. Marked uncertain only because the user explicitly accepted the current Se_on loudness ('LINEAR (user-validated)'); the disasm itself unambiguously squares it.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/SpuVmKeyOnNow.c:47-48 squares the magnitude ((uVar8*uVar8)/0x3fff into SPU_CH_VOL_L/R). The Se_on (SCD 0x36) path provably reaches it: opcode jump-table entry 0x80074580 -> handler LAB_80041624 (ghidra1_V2.txt:151736), which at ghidra1_V2.txt:151814 does `jal FUN_80045024`. FUN_80045024.c:90 calls SsUtKeyOnV(...), and SsUtKeyOnV.c:84 calls `SpuVmKeyOnNow(1,uVar3)`. The squared magnitude (SpuVmKeyOnNow.c:13) is built from DAT_800b5318=voll * tone[0x18] * masterVol * progVol — i.e. the tone's vol, exactly the `tn->vol` our code uses. Our audio_psx.c:308-316 play_room_se computes spu_vol = vol*0x3FFF/127 LINEAR (no square). Our own footstep path se_vol_byte_true (audio_psx.c:323-329) already does (base*base)/0x3FFF for the SAME engine path, proving the square law applies to keyed SE. Note: the prior audit's named example callers FUN_8002c444/FUN_8004a0cc are NOT the Se_on handler (helicopter physics / BGM seq), but the real handler 0x80041624 does route through FUN_80045024 -> SsUtKeyOnV -> SpuVmKeyOnNow, so the conclusion holds.
verdict.fix_summary: In play_room_se (audio_psx.c:308-316) replace the linear `spu_vol = vol*0x3FFF/127` with the squared cascade already used for footsteps: spu = se_vol_byte_true(0x7f, tone_vol) = (((0x7f*0x3FFF/127)*tone_vol/127)^2)/0x3FFF, then apply the L/R pan split as now. This makes Se_on byte-true with SpuVmKeyOnNow's (mag^2)/0x3fff. CAVEAT: the existing comment 'LINEAR (user-validated)' plus auto-memory (se_on_global_room_bank_2026_06_18: 'Se_on stays LINEAR, user said it is fine') indicate the user has explicitly accepted the current louder Se_on. Since the project rule keeps a byte-true value only when visually/audibly NEUTRAL and reverts when it WORSENS the user-validated experience, confirm audibly with the user before applying; the disasm itself is unambiguous that it should be squared.

claim: ROOM1140 BGM is force-silenced via `if (stage==0 && room==0x14) return -1`, overriding the table value SS_BGMTBL[0x14]=0xff56 (=MAIN16). This is a hardcoded per-room override, not derived from the SCD/data. The table entry says MAIN16 should play.
disasm_cite: audio_psx.c's own SS_BGMTBL[20]=0xff56 (line 691) vs no Sce_bgm_control suppression in the room1140 SCD — the override is a user-validation patch, not a disasm-derived behavior
fix: none required (user-validated 2026-06-18 that the briefing room is silent and the table is a reconstructed-proto artifact). Flagged because it is a magic per-room constant in otherwise-generic table code; ideally find the real BGM gate (e.g. an SCD bgm-stop in room1140) to replace the hardcode.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80044210.c:8 (BGM id = &UNK_80074828 + _DAT_800b0fe2*2 + (&DAT_800748fc)[DAT_800b0fe0]*2 — pure room/stage table lookup, no room conditional); FUN_80044210.c:29 (FUN_80044564 = MAIN BGM load, called unconditionally); ghidra1_V2.txt:243621-243622 (table entry for room0x14: 0x80074850=0x56, 0x80074851=0xff -> LE 0xff56 = MAIN16); ghidra1_V2.txt:243796 (DAT_800748fc[0]=0x00 -> stage0 offset 0); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800396fc.c:61 (FUN_80044210 invoked unconditionally on room init). vs our psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/audio_psx.c:713 `if (stage==0 && room==0x14) return -1;`
verdict.fix_summary: The claim's FACTS are correct but its CATEGORY is wrong. Disasm proves the original engine plays SS_BGMTBL[0x14]=0xff56 (MAIN16) for room1140 unconditionally: FUN_80044210 indexes the table at UNK_80074828 by room/stage with NO per-room branch, and ghidra1_V2.txt:243621 confirms the room0x14 entry bytes are 56 ff (LE 0xff56). The room1140 SCD contains zero bgm-control opcodes (verified: no BGM op in extracted/.../room1140/scd). So our audio_psx.c:713 `return -1` is a HARDCODED behavioral override that contradicts the binary — by the strict definition this is a real deviation, NOT an engine_specific arch difference (it is not the SDL/synth/libsnd-less category; the PSX port is libsnd-based and would otherwise play MAIN16 identically). However, the byte-true fix (remove the override, let MAIN16 play) is intentionally NOT recommended: the user explicitly validated 2026-06-18 that the briefing room is silent, the SS_BGMTBL is a reconstructed-proto table whose 0xff56 likely is a proto artifact, and MAIN16 was masking the feeding-zombie eat SE. The honest classification is: confirmed deviation from disasm, kept as a documented user-validated override. To make it truly byte-true one would need to find a real data gate (e.g. a proto disc that nulls this entry, or an SCD bgm-stop) rather than the hardcode — that gate does not exist in the current data.

claim: Footstep/enemy pitch = re15_vab_note2pitch(tone.min_note, tone.center_note, tone.pitch_shift), matching the original note2pitch which uses (playedNote+0x3c-centerNote) and the DAT_80077520 Q12 LUT. Verified consistent: our s_pitch_lut is the byte-extracted DAT_80077520 and the octave shift / fine-step logic matches.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/note2pitch.c:7-21 (iVar1=(DAT_800b5316+0x3c-DAT_800b5324); LUT &DAT_80077520; octave shift (iVar1/0xc-5)) matches re15_vab_note2pitch in vab_common.c:211-223
fix: none — byte-true.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/SsUtKeyOnV.c:83 (the SE/footstep/enemy key-on path calls note2pitch2(note,fine), NOT note2pitch); ghidra1_V2.txt:184904-184906 (note2pitch2 does `v0 = a1(played fine) + tone[+5]` before `>>3`); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045024.c:90 (passes fine=*(iVar13+5)=tone pitch_shift, note=*(iVar13+6)=min_note) -> effective fine = 2*pitch_shift. Our re15_vab_note2pitch (psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/vab_common.c:214-215) uses fine16=pitch_shift/8 (1x). Refute-data: room1170 snd0.vh prog0 tones1-6 all pitch_shift=0 (so audit's room1170 result is identical, 0x800=22050 confirmed), BUT room1140 snd1.vh prog0 tones have pitch_shift=18/57/60 which the original doubles+carries and our code does not.
verdict.fix_summary: The audit's "ok" verdict is correct ONLY for the room1170 footstep scope (every footstep tone has pitch_shift=0, so 1x vs 2x fine is identical and yields the verified 0x800/22050). But the broad "enemy pitch matches note2pitch" part is refuted on two counts: (1) the cited function is wrong — the real SE/footstep/enemy key-on path is note2pitch2 (SsUtKeyOnV.c:83), not note2pitch; (2) note2pitch2 (ghidra1_V2.txt:184904) sums the played fine (= the tone's pitch_shift, passed by FUN_80045024.c:90 as iVar13+5) WITH the tone's own pitch_shift (tone[+5]) before the >>3, and carries the overflow into the semitone dividend. Our re15_vab_note2pitch only applies pitch_shift once (vab_common.c:214 fine16=pitch_shift/8). For tones with pitch_shift!=0 (e.g. room1140 enemy snd1 tones, shift 18/57/60) this gives the wrong pitch. Byte-true fix: in re15_vab_note2pitch compute fine_total = pitch_shift + pitch_shift (the played-fine equals the tone fine for tone-derived SE calls), i.e. combined = (2*pitch_shift) >> 3, then if combined >= 16 carry 1 into the semitone index (total += 192) and use (combined - 16) as the low fine, exactly as note2pitch2 does (carry when (fine_sum>>3) >= 0x10). Keep the negative-fine +7 rounding bias from note2pitch2 (addiu v0,v0,0x7 before sra by 3) for the negative branch.

claim: Se_on volume DRIFTS between ports: PC play_room_se_pc applies a `>> 1` 'headroom halve' (`int q15 = (vol * 0x4000 / 127) >> 1;`), making every SCD Se_on SFX play at HALF the level of the PSX port. The PSX play_room_se (audio_psx.c:311) computes `spu_vol = vol * 0x3FFF / 127` with NO halve, and the original FUN_80045024 / SpuVmKeyOnNow has no such division either. The `>>1` is an un-cited 'tuned' magic that breaks PC↔PSX Se_on parity.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045024.c:90 (SsUtKeyOnV keys the SE with the raw EDT tone vol — no halving) and the level chain RE_15_Quellcode_V2/SpuVmKeyOnNow.c:13 (`DAT_800b5318 * toneVol * 0x3fff / 0x3f01 * progVol * seqVol / 0x3f01`, no /2)
fix: Drop the `>> 1` in play_room_se_pc: use `int q15 = vol * 0x4000 / 127;` (mirror audio_psx.c:311 which uses the full 0x3FFF scale). If a global PC headroom reduction is truly needed because several voices overlap-clip, it must be applied symmetrically (same factor) on the PSX side too, or cited — but the byte-true original applies none, so removing it is correct.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/SpuVmKeyOnNow.c:13 (level cascade `DAT_800b5318*toneVol*0x3fff/0x3f01*progVol*seqVol/0x3f01` — NO /2) and RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045024.c:90 (SsUtKeyOnV keys the SE with EDT vol from iVar13+2 / FUN_80045a64, no halving). Our two ports: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/audio_psx.c:311 `spu_vol = vol*0x3FFF/127` (vol=127 -> 0x3FFF) vs psx_dev/re15_reborn_pc/src/audio_pc.c:383 `q15 = (vol*0x4000/127) >> 1` (vol=127 -> 0x2000). The engine's own established scale mapping is proven by the IDENTICAL pair se_vol_byte_true (audio_psx.c:326-328) and se_vol_byte_true_pc (audio_pc.c:396-398), both returning 0..0x3FFF into the same SPU/mixer slot (audio_pc.c:261 mixes vol_l at >>15) — so PSX SPU_CH_VOL 0x3FFF maps to PC vol_l 0x3FFF. Therefore the PC Se_on emits 0x2000 where PSX emits 0x3FFF = exactly half.
verdict.fix_summary: Drop the `>> 1` in play_room_se_pc (audio_pc.c:383) so it mirrors the PSX play_room_se: `int q15 = vol * 0x3FFF / 127;` (use 0x3FFF, matching audio_psx.c:311 and the engine's own 0x3FFF SE ceiling, not 0x4000). The `>>1` is an un-cited "headroom halve (overlap)" tuning magic; the byte-true original (SpuVmKeyOnNow / SsUtKeyOnV) applies no such division. If overlap-clipping genuinely needs headroom, it must be applied symmetrically on the PSX side too (it currently is not) or cited — but the original applies none, so removing it restores PC<->PSX Se_on parity.

claim: The header/comment states `e[1] & 0x7f` is the VAB program and `bit7 of e[1]` is the alternate-bank flag. The disasm shows the alternate-bank/vab override is taken from `edt[0]` bit7 (`*pbVar10 & 0x80` -> vabId = `*pbVar10 & 0x7f`), while `edt[1]&0x7f` is the program (bit7 of edt[1] is unused). Our CODE reads program from e[1]&0x7f (correct) and never consults e[0], so the only error is the documentation locating the bank-flag on the wrong byte; the cross-bank override (edt[0] bit7) is also unimplemented.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045024.c:66-72 (`if ((*pbVar10 & 0x80)!=0) uVar11 = *pbVar10 & 0x7f;` -> bank from edt[0]; `bVar1 = pbVar10[1]` -> `prog = bVar1 & 0x7f`); RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045630.c:53 (`uVar10 = *(iVar8+1) & 0x7f` program)
fix: Fix the comment to say the alternate-bank flag is edt[0] bit7 (not edt[1]). For full cross-bank SE support, have re15_se_resolve also read e[0]: if `(e[0] & 0x80)` select the alternate VAB given by `e[0] & 0x7f` instead of the passed `vab`. Only matters for SE that reference a non-room bank (system/CORE); the footstep/room-SE path (program-addressed in one bank) is byte-true.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045024.c:65-68 (`pbVar10 = puVar9 + uVar16*4; if ((*pbVar10 & 0x80)!=0) uVar11 = *pbVar10 & 0x7f;` => alternate-bank/vabId from edt[0] bit7) and FUN_80045024.c:72,81 (`bVar1 = pbVar10[1]; prog = (short)(bVar1 & 0x7f);` => program from edt[1]&0x7f, edt[1] bit7 never tested = unused). Footstep path FUN_80045630.c:50-54 reads program at `*(iVar8+1)&0x7f` and uses a FIXED vabId `DAT_800b21ee` (no edt[0] consult). Our comment psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/vab_common.c:171-173 states e[1]'s bit7 is the alternate-bank flag; our code re15_se_resolve (vab_common.c:176) correctly reads program=e[1]&0x7f but never reads e[0].
verdict.fix_summary: The claim is CONFIRMED but low-impact. Part 1 (comment error): the comment at vab_common.c:171-173 mislocates the alternate-bank flag onto e[1] bit7; the disasm (FUN_80045024.c:66) puts it on edt[0] bit7, with edt[1] bit7 genuinely unused. Fix the comment to: 'e[1]&0x7f is the program; bit7 of e[1] is unused. The alternate-bank override is edt[0] bit7 -> vabId = edt[0]&0x7f (FUN_80045024.c:66-68).' Part 2 (unimplemented cross-bank override): real latent gap but NOT a behavioral deviation in any demonstrated path -- the footstep path FUN_80045630 never reads edt[0] bit7 (uses fixed vabId), so re15_audio_footstep is byte-true; only the general Se_on path (FUN_80045024) honors edt[0] bit7, and per prior RE room1170/STAGE1 fires no cross-bank Se_on (no failing case cited). Optional hardening: in play_se_on, if (s_foot_edt[sample_id*4]&0x80) the target VAB is edt[0]&0x7f -- a non-room bank that is not resident, so byte-true behavior there would still require loading that bank; current silent-return is acceptable until a real cross-bank SE is found. Recommend fixing the comment (trivial) and leaving the code, flagging edt[0] cross-bank as future work.

loc: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/vab_common.c:351 caller (audio_psx.c:243/292, audio_pc.c:351) — note value passed to note2pitch
claim: Footstep playback in the original uses note = `min_note + (rand & 2)` (a 0-or-2 semitone jitter added to the tone's min_note). re15_se_resolve returns the tone and the callers pass `tn->min_note` straight into re15_vab_note2pitch with NO `+ (rand & 2)` jitter, so every footstep plays at a fixed pitch instead of the slight per-step random detune.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045630.c:70-71 (`uVar5 = FUN_8001af20(); *(DAT_800b242a+iVar7) = (ushort)*(byte*)(iVar8+6) + (uVar5 & 2);` — min_note(+6) plus rng&2 stored as the play note)
fix: Outside this file (the jitter belongs in the audio caller), but note it: in audio_psx.c/audio_pc.c footstep play, compute `note = tn->min_note + (re15_rand() & 2)` and pass that to re15_vab_note2pitch instead of bare tn->min_note. re15_se_resolve / note2pitch themselves are correct; this is a missing caller term. FUN_8001af20 is the engine RNG.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80045630.c:70-71 (uVar5=FUN_8001af20(); *(DAT_800b242a+iVar7)=(ushort)*(byte*)(iVar8+6)+(uVar5&2) — min_note(tone byte 6) + rng&2 stored as the play note). FUN_80045630 is the FOOTSTEP play fn: it calls the floor.flr lookup FUN_800437d4 (FUN_80045630.c:19) and our code maps it (audio_psx.c:346 comment 'byte-true FUN_80045630'). It is the ONLY of the 3 SE-play fns adding the jitter — the sibling non-footstep SE fns store bare min_note: FUN_80045024.c:115 and FUN_800453d0.c:57 both '=(ushort)*(byte*)(iVar+6)' with NO +(rand&2). FUN_8001af20.c:1-10 = the engine byte-PRNG. Our footstep paths pass bare tn->min_note: PSX se_key_voice (audio_psx.c:292) via re15_audio_footstep (audio_psx.c:354-360); PC se_activate_pc via re15_audio_footstep (audio_pc.c:1476-1484, pitch at audio_pc.c:351/419 uses tn->min_note). No '& 2' jitter anywhere.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation: original footsteps detune the note by 0-or-2 (min_note + (rng&2)); our footstep play uses a fixed min_note, so every footstep plays at the same pitch. Fix in the FOOTSTEP CALLER ONLY (re15_audio_footstep in audio_psx.c:350 and audio_pc.c:1476), not in se_key_voice/se_activate_pc/note2pitch (those are shared by door/Se_on SE which use FUN_80045024/FUN_800453d0 = NO jitter per FUN_80045024.c:115 / FUN_800453d0.c:57; adding jitter there would wrongly detune non-footstep SE). Concretely: when keying a footstep, compute note = tn->min_note + (re15_rand() & 2) and feed that note into re15_vab_note2pitch (e.g. add a note-override param to the keyer, or make a footstep-local copy of the tone with min_note bumped). re15_rand must be the engine PRNG (port of FUN_8001af20) so the sequence stays byte-true. re15_se_resolve and re15_vab_note2pitch are already byte-true. NOTE the claim's loc line numbers (243/292/351) point at the SHARED keyer — apply the fix in the footstep caller, not those lines.

claim: DISTANCE FADE — vertical (Y) term is wrong. Our code computes the standard 3D distance dy = cam_eye[1]-heli_pos[1] and uses dy*dy. The original does NOT use dy; it squares (cam.Y - |cam.Y - src.Y|). Trace of the asm: s0 = |cam.Y - src.Y| (80045aec/af4/b00), then v1 = cam.Y - s0 (80045b18 reloads cam.Y, 80045b20 subu v1,v1,s0), then v1*v1 (80045b24). So the vertical contribution is (cam.Y - |dy|)^2, dominated by cam.Y's absolute magnitude, NOT dy^2. Since PSX cam.Y is large (e.g. -1000s), this changes the fade distance massively (e.g. cam.Y=-1000,src.Y=-1200: original term=(-1200)^2=1.44e6 vs our dy^2=4e4).
fix: Replace the Y term: instead of dy=cam_eye[1]-heli_pos[1], compute ady = llabs((int64_t)cam_eye[1]-heli_pos[1]); int64_t vy = (int64_t)cam_eye[1] - ady; and use vy*vy in the sum. i.e. r = isqrt(vy*vy + (dx*dx + dz*dz)) with the horizontal term computed as in the next finding.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:157806 (80045ae0 lw s0,0x8(s2)=cam.Y), :157811 (80045af4 subu s0=cam.Y-src.Y), :157814 (80045b00 abs → s0=|dy|), :157821 (80045b18 lw v1,0x8(s2)=cam.Y reload), :157823 (80045b20 subu v1,v1,s0 = cam.Y-|dy|), :157824 (80045b24 mult v1,v1 = (cam.Y-|dy|)²). C-decompile FUN_80045a64.c:33 (iVar5 = *(iVar12+8) - iVar5; where iVar5=|cam.Y-src.Y|) and :34 (SquareRoot0(iVar5*iVar5 + lVar7*lVar8)). Our deviating code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/rotor_common.c:102 (dy = cam_eye[1]-heli_pos[1]) + :104 (dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz).
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation, cannot refute. Both the raw MIPS asm (80045ae0-80045b24) and the C decompile (FUN_80045a64.c:33-34) compute the vertical term as (cam.Y - |cam.Y - src.Y|)², NOT dy². Our rotor_common.c:102-104 uses plain dy=cam_eye[1]-heli_pos[1] and dy*dy. For PSX cameras (cam.Y typically large-magnitude, e.g. -1000s), cam.Y - |dy| ≈ cam.Y, so the original's vertical contribution is dominated by cam.Y's absolute magnitude (~cam.Y²), wildly different from dy² (e.g. cam.Y=-1000,src.Y=-1200 → original (-1200)²=1.44e6 vs ours (-200)²=4e4). This directly skews r=isqrt(...), di=r/250, and thus the SE_ATTEN applied to both rotor channels → wrong distance fade. Fix at rotor_common.c:102-104: ady = llabs((int64_t)cam_eye[1] - heli_pos[1]); vy = (int64_t)cam_eye[1] - ady; then r = rotor_isqrt((uint64_t)(vy*vy + dx*dx + dz*dz)). Not an accepted RE2 arch difference — it's a porting bug in byte-true audio attenuation.

claim: DISTANCE FADE — horizontal term loses precision in the original but ours keeps it exact. The original computes h = SquareRoot0(dx^2+dz^2) TWICE (lVar7,lVar8) and multiplies them: lVar7*lVar8 = floor(sqrt(dx^2+dz^2))^2, which is <= dx^2+dz^2 (drops the fractional part of the sqrt). Our code uses the exact dx*dx+dz*dz. Final dist = sqrt(verticalTerm + floor(sqrt(dx^2+dz^2))^2), so our distance is slightly larger than the original near non-perfect-square horizontals, shifting the SE_ATTEN index by up to 1.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:157816-157829 (80045b04 SquareRoot0(s1); 80045b10 SquareRoot0(s1) again; 80045b2c mult s1,v0 = h*h; 80045b38 addu a0,v1,a0; 80045b34 SquareRoot0) ; FUN_80045a64.c:31-34
fix: Compute hsq = dx*dx+dz*dz; uint32_t h = rotor_isqrt(hsq); then dist = rotor_isqrt((uint64_t)vy*vy + (uint64_t)h*(uint64_t)h); — i.e. round-trip the horizontal distance through isqrt before re-squaring, matching the double-SquareRoot0.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:157816-157838 and FUN_80045a64.c:31-35. Original: lVar7=SquareRoot0(iVar6) (80045b04), lVar8=SquareRoot0(iVar6) (80045b10) where iVar6=dx*dx+dz*dz (horizontal sq-sum); then mult s1,v0 = lVar7*lVar8 = floor(sqrt(dx2+dz2))^2 (80045b2c); addu a0,v1,a0 = dy2 + that (80045b38); SquareRoot0 -> dist (80045b34); /0xfa (250) -> SE_ATTEN idx (80045b3c-44). So original radicand = dy2 + floor(sqrt(dx2+dz2))^2. Our rotor_common.c:104 does rotor_isqrt(dx*dx+dy*dy+dz*dz) i.e. radicand dy2+dx2+dz2 with the horizontal term kept EXACT. Since floor(sqrt(dx2+dz2))^2 <= dx2+dz2, our dist >= original near non-perfect-square horizontals, shifting the di index (and thus SE_ATTEN-attenuated L/R volume) by up to 1. The cite is real and the math is exactly as claimed.
verdict.fix_summary: Restore the original's double-sqrt structure at rotor_common.c:104. Compute the horizontal distance through isqrt FIRST, then re-square it before adding the vertical term: uint32_t h = rotor_isqrt((uint64_t)(dx*dx + dz*dz)); uint32_t r = rotor_isqrt((uint64_t)dy*dy + (uint64_t)h*h);. This matches lVar7*lVar8 = floor(sqrt(dx2+dz2))^2. Note: rotor_isqrt is exact-floor, which is the project-wide accepted model for PSX SquareRoot0 (same as the outer isqrt already used at :104, and re15_isqrt/cam_isqrt/zb_isqrt elsewhere) — SquareRoot0 itself is actually a 192-entry-table approximation (PSn00bSDK squareroot.s), but modeling it with exact floor-isqrt is the established codebase convention, so the proposed fix is consistent. The structural floor-then-square of the horizontal term is the genuine deviation being fixed.

claim: OUTPUT VOLUME — clamp instead of mask. Our code does panL -= SE_ATTEN(di); if(>0x7f)=0x7f; if(<0)=0. The original does (value - atten) & 0x7f (subu then andi 0x7f), a WRAP not a clamp. This differs sharply for the 0x89 'near full' channel: original yields (0x89 - atten) & 0x7f (e.g. atten=0 -> 0x89&0x7f = 0x09, very quiet), while our code clamps 0x89-0 to 0x7f (full). For the in-range pan curve (<=0x7f) the results coincide, but the dead-ahead/behind (both 0x89) and the near channel of an off-axis source diverge.
fix: Replace both clamps with a mask to match byte-true: panL = (panL - (int)SE_ATTEN(di)) & 0x7f; panR = (panR - (int)SE_ATTEN(di)) & 0x7f; (drop the >0x7f and <0 clamps). The 0x89 literal must survive into the subtract unclamped so the &0x7f wrap occurs exactly as in the asm.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:157956 (80045d08 subu v0,v0,v1) → 157957 (80045d0c sh v0 stores RAW unclamped diff) → 157964 (80045d28 andi v0,v0,0x7f) → 157965 (sh masked). Identical for R channel: 157966 (80045d30 subu v1,v1,a1), 157969 (80045d3c andi v1,v1,0x7f). No slti/sltu/branch clamp exists between subu and andi. Decompile FUN_80045a64.c:72-73 confirms: `DAT_800b2824 = DAT_800b2824 - atten & 0x7f` (C precedence: (val-atten)&0x7f). The 0x89 near value reaches the subtract via .c:55-56 (dead-ahead/behind both=0x89) and .c:69-70 (off-axis near side = bVar1*0x89). Our rotor_common.c:130-131 clamps [0,0x7f] instead of masking: original (0x89-0)&0x7f=0x09 (quiet) vs our clamp→0x7f (full); original (0x89-0x09)&0x7f=0x00 vs our→0x7f. Diverges on every 0x89 channel.
verdict.fix_summary: Replace the clamps at rotor_common.c:130-131 with byte-true masks: `panL = (panL - (int)SE_ATTEN(di)) & 0x7f; panR = (panR - (int)SE_ATTEN(di)) & 0x7f;` (drop the >0x7f and <0 clamps). The 0x89 near value must reach the subtract unclamped so the &0x7f wrap reproduces the original's (e.g. 0x89→0x09) quiet-near behavior. Note the original stores the raw diff to DAT_800b2824/26 first (80045d0c/d2c) then re-stores the masked value, but only the final masked value is consumed downstream, so a single masked assignment is equivalent.

claim: AZIMUTH — our rotor_catan uses a hand-built ATAN256[257] LUT plus a 90-degree complement, presented as 'the equivalent of the BIOS catan'. The original calls the actual PSY-Q library catan (jal catan @80045db4 inside FUN_80045d6c), which uses its own LUT (DAT_80078c90). The two are not guaranteed bit-identical; at angle/octant boundaries the (rel&0x3ff)>>3 pan index could differ by 1. Cannot prove byte-equality without disassembling catan's LUT, so flagged rather than asserted.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:158015 (80045db4 jal catan) and 206403 (int catan(int a)) ; FUN_80045d6c.c:23 (catan(iVar3/param_3))
fix: Disassemble catan @0x80064xxx (entry near 206403 in ghidra1_V2.txt, LUT DAT_80078c90) and either reproduce its exact LUT/algorithm or extract the 256/512-entry table verbatim, then verify rotor_catan output matches catan for all inputs used (rel in 0..0xfff). Until verified, treat the pan index as approximate.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:206403-206494 (catan = 12-iteration CORDIC, loop bound slti v0,a2,0xc @206488, table DAT_80078c90 read @206456); ghidra1_V2.txt:261067-261099 (DAT_80078c90 = {0x1FF,0x12E,0x9F,0x51,0x29,0x14,0x0A,0x05,0x03,0x01,0x00,0x00} = CORDIC atan increments on a 0x1000=360deg circle); FUN_80045d6c.c:23 (azimuth calls catan(iVar3/param_3)); our code rotor_common.c:47-75 (ATAN256[257] direct LUT). Numerically faithful CORDIC reproduction (validated vs math.atan2: z=512->81, z=4096->511) vs our ATAN256 LUT: MAXDIFF=7, differs on 595173/600001 inputs; end-to-end the SE_PAN pan index pi=(rel&0x3ff)>>3 differs on 58584/200000 (29%) random cam/heli geometries (e.g. cam[-15662,_,19869] tgt[120,_,-11009] heli[-28592,_,-2726] -> CORDIC pi=81 vs LUT pi=80).
verdict.fix_summary: Replace rotor_catan's ATAN256[257] direct LUT (rotor_common.c:47-75) with the exact PsyQ CORDIC used by catan @0x800658fc: x=0x1000, y=z, ang=0; for i in 0..11 (CT={0x1FF,0x12E,0x9F,0x51,0x29,0x14,0x0A,0x05,0x03,0x01,0,0}): if y>=0 {x+=y>>i; y-=x_old>>i; ang+=CT[i]} else {x-=y>>i; y+=x_old>>i; ang-=CT[i]} using arithmetic (MIPS srav) shifts; return ang. Keep rotor_azimuth feeding it the truncating-division ratio ((p4-p2)<<12)/dx (MIPS integer div truncates toward zero, not floor) exactly as FUN_80045d6c.c:16,23. This makes the rotor stereo pan index byte-identical to the original; the LUT path is provably off by up to 7 catan-units and flips the SE_PAN index on ~29% of geometries. Cosmetic/audio-only (rotor SUB-layer L/R balance), but a real byte deviation.

claim: RE15_PRI_MAX_MASKS_PER_CUT = 64 silently DROPS masks. The parse loop guards `if (out_count < RE15_PRI_MAX_MASKS_PER_CUT)` (pri_common.c:116) so any cut with >64 foreground occluders loses the surplus. Real data exceeds this: room1190 has a cut with 79 masks (verified parsing extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1190/sprite.pri); even room1170 cut-5 already has 56. Masks 65..N never get a re15_pri_mask_t and never draw -> missing foreground occlusion in those rooms.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800392d4.c:49-105 — the mask loop is bounded ONLY by `(uVar8 & 0xff) < (ushort)*puVar11` (the per-group count) summed over `bVar5 < uVar21` groups; there is NO 64-element cap. Every mask in sum(group_n) is written to the priority buffer DAT_800b2584 (pbVar19 += 4 per mask).
fix: Raise RE15_PRI_MAX_MASKS_PER_CUT to comfortably exceed the observed max — set it to 128 (max real = 79). Update the masks[] array dimension in re15_pri_cut_t accordingly. (The PSX original buffer DAT_800b2584 is sized for the room's worst-case; 128 covers every extracted room.)

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800392d4.c:38-110 — the original sprite.pri renderer has NO 64-element cap. Outer loop `do {...} while (bVar5 < uVar21)` iterates over ALL groups (uVar21 = group_count, line 110); inner loop `do {...} while ((uVar8 & 0xff) < (ushort)*puVar11)` (line 105) iterates over EVERY mask in the group (*puVar11 = per-group count). Each mask unconditionally writes the priority buffer DAT_800b2584 (`*pbVar19 |= 1`, line 57; `pbVar19 += 4`, line 78) and two OT quad prims (iVar9/iVar12, += 0x20 each, lines 103-104). No comparison against 64 exists anywhere in the function. Our code caps at RE_15_Quellcode... wait — our cap is in psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/pri_common.c:116 `if (out_count < RE15_PRI_MAX_MASKS_PER_CUT)` (=64, include/re15_pri.h:38), and both consumers honor it (PC main.c:957 clamps n→64; PSX render.c:444 iterates the capped mask_count and DOES draw each as a real SPRT, lines 455-468 — NOT disabled, the pri_common.c "overdraw disabled" comment is stale). Real data exceeds 64: extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1190/sprite.json sections at lines 2304 & 3509 declare AND parse 79 masks; room1210/room1211 (room1210/sprite.json:3924-3925) declare AND parse 149 masks (68 groups). Masks 64..N are dropped → missing foreground occlusion.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation confirmed, BUT the proposed fix value (128) is WRONG/too small. The global max across all extracted rooms is 149 masks (room1210/1211 section 6, 68 groups — verified maskCountDeclared==maskCountParsed==149 in extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1210/sprite.json:3924), and room1190/1191 have 79. 128 would still drop 21 masks in room1210. Byte-true fix: raise RE15_PRI_MAX_MASKS_PER_CUT (re15_pri.h:38) to cover the architectural ceiling. The declared count is the PSX byte `(char)(uVar2>>0x10)` (FUN_800392d4.c:35), max 255, and our parser already permits mask_count_decl up to 256 (pri_common.c:50) — so set the cap to 256 to match that ceiling exactly (clean and never drops). At minimum it must be >=160. This resizes masks[] in re15_pri_cut_t and the PC stack arrays in main.c:952-955 (re15_pri_cut_t is heap/static, ~256*16B=4KB — fine; PC's 7 stack int[] arrays of 256 = ~7KB stack, acceptable). Note the existing pri_common.c:50 guard already gates malformed sections at 256.

claim: Parser is MASK-DRIVEN (loops mask_count_decl = hi-u16, advancing a group cursor), but the original is GROUP-DRIVEN (outer loop over groups, inner loop over each group's own count). hi-u16 (uVar2>>16) is NOT the parse bound in the original — it is only the OT-EMIT count used later by FUN_80039590. These diverge when hi-u16 != sum(group_n): verified in room1210/1211 sec4 (hi=75 vs sum=77). The original parses 77 masks into the priority buffer and emits the first 75; ours parses exactly 75 and leaves the group cursor mid-group. Net pixels match here by luck, but the parse model is not byte-true.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800392d4.c:49-110 (group-driven inner loop `while ((uVar8 & 0xff) < (ushort)*puVar11)`) vs RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80039590.c:9,12,23 where `bVar1 = *DAT_800ac778` (= the stored hi-u16, FUN_800392d4.c:35 `*DAT_800ac778 = (char)(uVar2 >> 0x10)`) is the SEPARATE emit-count loop bound.
fix: Restructure the loop to iterate groups outer / group_n[i] inner (consume sum(group_n) masks), and record the hi-u16 separately as the emit count (cap rendering to the first hi-u16 masks). i.e. parse `sum(group_n)` masks, store all, and have the renderer stop after `mask_count_decl` of them.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_800392d4.c:110 (outer loop `while (bVar5 < uVar21)`, uVar21 = uVar2 & 0xffff = LOW u16 = group_count, set at :32) + FUN_800392d4.c:105 (inner loop `while ((uVar8 & 0xff) < (ushort)*puVar11)`, *puVar11 = each group's OWN mask count, group ptr advanced +8 bytes at :109) + FUN_800392d4.c:35 (`*DAT_800ac778 = (char)(uVar2 >> 0x10)` stores the HIGH u16 separately) + FUN_80039590.c:9,23 (`bVar1 = *DAT_800ac778` is the SEPARATE emit/AddPrim loop bound). Our pri_common.c:75 loops the HIGH u16 (mask_count_decl, read at :42) as the parse bound and uses a group cursor at :108-114 — structurally mask-driven, not group-driven. The disasm cite is real and accurate; the structural deviation is confirmed.
verdict.fix_summary: The audit's STRUCTURE cite is correct: the original is group-driven (outer over group_count=low-u16, inner over each group's own count, total parsed = sum(group_n)) and emits only the first hi-u16 (FUN_80039590), whereas ours is mask-driven (loops hi-u16 with a group cursor). BUT the audit's IMPACT claim ('pixels match by luck', 'cursor left mid-group') is REFUTED: I parsed the real bytes of every room. Of 480 sections, 478 have hi == sum(group_n) (identical output by definition); exactly 2 diverge (room1210/1211 cut 4: hi=75, sum=77, group[32]=0, group[33]=8). For those 2 I compared mask-by-mask: the original emits the first 75 masks (anchors+dst), ours parses exactly those same 75 — 0 mismatches; the 2 masks the original parses-but-never-emits are precisely the 2 ours never parses. It is a structural identity, not luck. The only model-divergent failure mode (hi > sum, where the original would index unparsed buffer slots) occurs in 0/480 sections game-wide. So: REAL mechanism deviation (not byte-true at the parse level), but with provably ZERO observable effect on any actual asset and no current path to one. If a byte-true refactor is wanted regardless, the audit's fix is the right shape: outer-loop groups, inner-loop group_n[i] (parse sum(group_n) masks, group[i]=0 simply contributes none — matches the :51 `if (*puVar11 != 0)` skip), and cap the renderer to the first mask_count_decl masks (store mask_count_decl separately as the emit count). Low priority — visually neutral across 100% of data.

claim: Stair gait 220->PL00 clip 21 (descend) / 221->clip 20 (ascend). Clip 21 IS cited; clip 20 (ascend) is asserted by the comment as 'USER-CONFIRMED' which is not a disasm cite.
disasm_cite: stair_common.c:6-9 cites LAB_80038c60 phase0 sets clip DAT_800acae8=0x15 (=21) for descend; the ascend handler LAB_80038850 (stair_common.c:6) is the cite-source for clip 20 — verify it writes DAT_800acae8=0x14.
fix: Read LAB_80038850 (ascend) in ghidra1_V2.txt and confirm it sets the clip to 0x14 (=20). If so, drop the 'USER-CONFIRMED' justification and cite the address; if it sets a different clip, fix the 221->20 mapping accordingly.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:136465-136467 (ascend LAB_80038850 phase-0 writes clip 0x5: `ori v0,zero,0x5` then `sb v0,...=>DAT_800acae8`); vs ghidra1_V2.txt:136747-136749 (descend LAB_80038c60 phase-0 writes clip 0x15: `ori v0,zero,0x15` -> DAT_800acae8). Both handlers are the FSM stair pair, dispatched from ghidra1_V2.txt:242057-242058 (8007401c->LAB_80038850 ascend, 80074020->LAB_80038c60 descend). Our wrong mapping: anim_select_common.c:218 (and stair_common.c:186) use 20 for ascend.
verdict.fix_summary: The audit correctly flagged that ascend clip 20 was an uncited 'USER-CONFIRMED' guess, but its proposed value (0x14=20) is ALSO wrong. The ascend handler LAB_80038850 phase-0 (ghidra1_V2.txt:136465) sets DAT_800acae8 = 0x5 (ori v0,zero,0x5; sb v0->DAT_800acae8), i.e. PL00 clip 5, NOT 0x14/20. Fix: anim_select_common.c:218 change the ascend branch `(m==220)?21:20` to `(m==220)?21:5`, and stair_common.c:186 change `?21:20` to `?21:5`, citing 80038cb4(=0x15 descend) and 800388a4(=0x5 ascend). Update the now-stale comments at anim_select_common.c:212 and stair_common.c:60/186/205/352 that assert 'ascend=clip 20'. The descend=21 mapping remains correct and is properly cited.

loc: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/scd_vm.c:2224 (op_sce_em_set); enemy_common.c is only the pool table — the actual spawn handler the FOCUS names lives here)
claim: Sce_em_set (0x44) is implemented as an UNCONDITIONAL spawn — every actor is activated. The original is a CONDITIONAL spawn gated on a game-flag byte: pc[7] (spawn_flag). If pc[7]==0xFF -> always spawn; otherwise FUN_8004efe4 tests bit pc[7] in a flag bitfield and, if the flag is SET, the entity is SUPPRESSED (no spawn, instead FUN_80039b2c(pc[5]) runs). For the room1170 crows pc[7]=0x32..0x38 (NOT 0xFF) and Elliot pc[7]=0xFF, so the 7 crows are each conditional on their own progress flag while Elliot is unconditional — our code spawns all 8 regardless.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:152530 (lbu a1,0x7(a1)=pc[7]); :152532 (beq pc[7]==0xff -> spawn); :152534 (jal FUN_8004efe4 flag test); :152536-152544 (flag-set -> suppress / s1=pc[1]&0x80 bail); FUN_8004efe4.c:4 (bit test 0x80000000>>(bit&0x1f) & flags[bit>>5])
fix: Before activating the actor: read fl=pc[7]; if (fl!=0xFF) { uint base-bank flags = the SCD/game flag bitfield used by FUN_8004efe4 (a3 = DAT_800b1038 normally, DAT_800b1058 when the scenario word DAT_800b0fe0>=3); if (flag_bit(fl) set) -> do NOT spawn (optionally run the pc[5] callback) and if (pc[1]&0x80) also skip; } Only then set a->active=1. Wire pc[7] to the existing g_game.flags bank so post-progress crows/enemies disappear byte-true.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:152530 (lbu a1,0x7(a1)=pc[7]); :152531-152532 (ori v0,0xff; beq pc[7]==0xff -> LAB_8004215c unconditional spawn); :152534-152535 (jal FUN_8004efe4, a0=flag-bank a3); :152536 (beq v0,zero -> continue); :152538-152541 (flag SET -> lbu +0x1c6, jal FUN_80039b2c, j LAB_80042620 = NO spawn); :152544 (bne s1,zero -> bail; s1=pc[1]&0x80 from :152520). Handler is dispatch idx 0x44: PTR_LAB_800744a8 + 68*4 = 800745b8 -> addr LAB_800420a0 (ghidra1_V2.txt:243105, :152496). FUN_8004efe4.c:4 = bit-test 0x80000000>>(idx&0x1f) & flags[idx>>5]. Bank a3 = DAT_800b1038, or DAT_800b1058 when DAT_800b0fe0>=3 (ghidra1_V2.txt:152511,152528,152535). pc[7]=enemyKilledId per RE15Editor Enemy_sceEmSet_44.java:34-35. Real room1170 sub15.scd: Elliot(0x47) pc[7]=0xFF, 7 crows(0x21) pc[7]=0x32..0x38. Our code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/scd_vm.c:2247-2248 sets a->active=1 unconditionally, never reads pc[7]/tests a flag bank/honors pc[1]&0x80.
verdict.fix_summary: In op_sce_em_set (scd_vm.c:2247, shared by PSX+PC), before a->active=1: read fl=pc[7]; if (fl!=0xFF) { pick flag zone = scenario-word>=3 ? bank_B : bank_A (mirror DAT_800b1038 vs DAT_800b1058 selection on DAT_800b0fe0>=3); if (re15_game_flag_get(zone, fl)) { /* flag SET -> SUPPRESS: optionally run the pc[5]/+0x1c6 callback FUN_80039b2c equivalent */ skip spawn; return; } if (pc[1]&0x80) { skip spawn; return; } }. Only then activate. The flag-bank API already exists (re15_game_flag_get in game_state.c = exact FUN_8004efe4 mirror); wire pc[7] to it so post-progress crows/enemies disappear byte-true while Elliot (pc[7]=0xFF) always spawns. Caveat: replicate the scenario-word a3 bank selection rather than a fixed zone.

claim: Spawn pose (+0x94 motion) is derived ENTIRELY from a hand-built behavior->clip decode table. The EXE-level handler, AFTER the overlay's per-type init runs, OVERWRITES +0x94 with the literal LE16 from the opcode at pc[18..19] whenever pc[18..19]!=0. So when a script authors a nonzero pc[18] the literal wins over any decode. Our handler never reads pc[18..19] at all. (Room1170 crows have pc[18..19]==0, so for that room the write is skipped and the result is visually neutral — but the mechanism is missing for any enemy authored with pc[18]!=0.)
fix: In op_sce_em_set, after seeding the overlay/decode default: int16_t init_act = scd_read_le_s16(&t->pc[18]); if (init_act != 0) a->motion = (uint8_t)init_act; (mirror the original's pc[18]!=0 gate).

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:152518 (s2 = a1+2 = scriptPC+2, where a1=lw 0x1c(s5)=thread PC @152515); ghidra1_V2.txt:152827 (jalr v0 = per-type overlay init via dispatch table 0x80072bac, seeds entity+0x94 FIRST); ghidra1_V2.txt:152829 (lh v0,0x10(s2) = signed pc[18..19]); ghidra1_V2.txt:152832 (beq v0,zero,LAB_800425fc = skip when pc[18..19]==0); ghidra1_V2.txt:152833 (_move s1,v0); ghidra1_V2.txt:152842 (sb s1,0x94(s0) = low byte of pc[18..19] OVERWRITES motion AFTER overlay init). Our code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/scd_vm.c:2251 sets a->motion from re15_enemy_spawn_action() only and never reads pc[18..19] (op reads pc[1..3],pc[8..13],pc[16..17] only). actor field confirmed: include/re15_actor.h:81 motion = ID8 motion_no/clip id = engine analog of entity+0x94.
verdict.fix_summary: Could not refute. The cite is real and accurate: s2 = scriptPC+2 (ghidra1_V2.txt:152518), so 0x10(s2) is exactly pc[18..19]; the EXE reads it signed, and when nonzero writes its low byte to entity+0x94 AFTER the overlay's per-type init at the first jalr — overriding the decode default. Our op_sce_em_set never reads pc[18..19]. Latent (room1170 crows have pc[18]==0 so visually neutral there, but the mechanism is missing for any enemy authored with pc[18]!=0). Proposed fix is byte-true for the motion field: after seeding the decode default, do `int16_t init_act = scd_read_le_s16(&t->pc[18]); if (init_act != 0) a->motion = (uint8_t)init_act;` (low byte matches the `sb`, the !=0 gate matches `beq v0,zero`). Two caveats for completeness (not strictly part of this finding): the same pc[18]!=0 branch also writes entity+0x1c4 (anim flags) from lhu 0xc(s2)=pc[14] (ghidra1_V2.txt:152843/152846) and re-dispatches the type handler via a second jalr (ghidra1_V2.txt:152849) — our code seeds anim_flags from a hardcoded settle check instead, and replicates no re-dispatch; a fully byte-true port would gate anim_flags on pc[18] too.

claim: anim_flags (+0x1c4) is set by a 'settle' heuristic (clear for clips 0x0C/0x0E/0x12/0x13, else 0x04). The original writes +0x1c4 = the LITERAL LE u16 from the opcode at pc[14..15], and only in the same pc[18]!=0 branch. There is no settle/loop-bit heuristic in the EXE handler.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:152843 (lhu v1,0xc(s2)=pc[14]); :152846 (sh v1,0x1c4(s0)=+0x1c4 anim_flags); guarded by the same :152832 pc[18]!=0 branch
fix: Replace the settle heuristic with: anim_flags is left as initialized (the overlay/+0x1c4 clear at :152551) UNLESS pc[18..15] non-zero branch is taken, in which case a->anim_flags = scd_read_le_u16(&t->pc[14]). For room1170 pc[14..15]==0 so anim_flags would stay 0; loop behaviour for crows then comes from the overlay/render side, not this opcode.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:152518 (addiu s2,a1,0x2 — s2 = pc+2, so all s2 offsets are pc-relative +2); ghidra1_V2.txt:152551 (sh zero,0x1c4(s0) — +0x1c4 init-cleared to 0); ghidra1_V2.txt:152829+152832 (lh v0,0x10(s2)=pc[18..19]; beq v0,zero,LAB_800425fc — guard); ghidra1_V2.txt:152843 (lhu v1,0xc(s2)=pc[14..15]); ghidra1_V2.txt:152846 (sh v1,0x1c4(s0) — literal copy into anim_flags). grep over the whole handler (0x80042020-0x800426xx) shows ONLY two writes to 0x1c4(s0): 152551 and 152846 — no settle/clip heuristic, no OR of a 0x04 bit. Semantics confirmed at ghidra1_V2.txt:177022-177027 (FUN_80050cb8 phase FSM: andi v0,0x4 on +0x1c4 = the LOOP gate our out->clip_override replicates). Our deviating code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/scd_vm.c:2257-2261.
verdict.fix_summary: REFUTATION FAILED — the finding is a genuine byte-true deviation. Our scd_vm.c:2257-2261 derives anim_flags via a fabricated 'settle' heuristic (clear for clips 0x0C/0x0E/0x12/0x13, else 0x04). The EXE handler FUN_800420a0 does NO such thing: it inits +0x1c4=0 (152551), then ONLY if pc[18..19]!=0 (branch 152832 on lh 0x10(s2), s2=pc+2 per 152518) writes the LITERAL LE u16 from pc[14..15] (152843 lhu 0xc(s2)) into +0x1c4 (152846 sh). The audit's offset labels (pc[14], pc[18]) are correct because s2=pc+2; the raw 0xc(s2)/0x10(s2) resolve to pc[14..15]/pc[18..19]. Concrete impact: room1170 crows are type 0x21 → re15_enemy_spawn_action returns 0 → settle=false → our code force-sets anim_flags=0x04 (always-loop), whereas byte-true it is pc[18]-gated and copied from pc[14..15] (0 for room1170 if pc[18..19]==0, with crow looping then coming from the overlay/render side, not this opcode). FIX (byte-true): replace lines 2257-2261 with `a->anim_flags = scd_read_le_u16(&t->pc[18]) ? scd_read_le_u16(&t->pc[14]) : 0;` — i.e. leave the 0-init unless pc[18..19]!=0, in which case anim_flags = LE u16 at pc[14..15]. Drop the settle/clip family entirely.

claim: We use the full byte pc[1] as the entity slot. The original masks it: slot index = pc[1] & 0x7f (multiplied by the 500-byte/0x1F4 entity stride), and the top bit pc[1] & 0x80 is a SEPARATE flag (s1) consumed by the spawn-gate (used to decide bail-vs-spawn when the progress flag is clear). Treating the whole byte as the slot would mis-index any entity authored with bit7 set.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:152519 (andi s4,v0,0x7f = slot index); :152520 (andi s1,v0,0x80 = separate flag); :152521-152527 (s0 = pool_base + s4*0x1F4); :152544 (bne s1,zero -> bail in the flag-clear path)
fix: Mask the slot: uint8_t slot = t->pc[1] & 0x7f; and capture uint8_t spawn_flag_bit = t->pc[1] & 0x80; to feed the conditional-spawn gate (finding #1). For room1170 all pc[1] are 0..6 so visually neutral, but byte-true requires the mask + the bit7 consumer.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:152519 (andi s4,v0,0x7f = slot index from pc[1]); :152520 (andi s1,v0,0x80 = separate bit7 flag); :152521-152527 (s0 = pool_base(a0) + s4*0x1F4, i.e. ((s4<<5)-s4)<<2 +s4, <<2 = 500-byte stride); :152530-152534 (lbu a1,pc[7]; cmp 0xff; jal FUN_8004efe4 spawn-gate); :152544 (bne s1,zero -> LAB_80042620 bail when bit7 set in the flag-clear path). Our code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/scd_vm.c:2226 `uint8_t slot = t->pc[1];` (no mask) and :1835 SCRIPT_SLOT_TO_ACTOR(s)=(s)+1 — bit7 never masked off nor consumed (grep for 0x7f mask in scd_vm.c = no matches).
verdict.fix_summary: Mask the slot to its low 7 bits and capture bit7 as a separate spawn-gate flag. At scd_vm.c:2226 use `uint8_t slot = t->pc[1] & 0x7f;` and `uint8_t spawn_flag_bit = t->pc[1] & 0x80;`. Then implement the gate matching :152530-152544: read the progress byte at pc[7]; if it equals 0xff OR the progress check (FUN_8004efe4) passes, spawn; otherwise (flag-clear path) bail without spawning when spawn_flag_bit is set, else spawn. This is visually neutral for room1170 (all authored slots 0..6, bit7 clear) but required for byte-true behavior of any entity authored with bit7 set, and to avoid mis-indexing the +1 actor slot.

claim: Member_set ids 6/7/8/9/10 are mistranslated. Real RE1.5 dispatcher FUN_8004116c maps id6->flags(+0x00,s32), id7->(+0x0c,s32), id8->u8(+0x01), id9->u8(+0x05), id10->u8(+0x06). Our re15_to_re2_member_id sends 6->0x06,7->0x07,8->0x08,9->0x09,10->0x0A; actor_common then does 0x06->sub_state_4, 0x07->no set-case (no-op), 0x08->re15_actor_set_motion (clip id), 0x09/0x0A->no-op. So id6 should set flags but sets sub_state_4; id8 should set u8@+0x01 but changes the animation clip; id7/9/10 silently dropped. Proven used: room1040/1041 sub05 Member_set(9,15)/Member_set(10,0) are dropped.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8004116c.c:28-41 (case6=*param_1=+0x00 flags, case7=param_1[3]=+0x0c, case8=(int)param_1+1, case9=+5, case10=+6); caller ghidra1_V2.txt:151257-151271 LAB_800410b8
fix: Port FUN_8004116c directly: id6->flags(+0x00), id7->+0x0c, id8->u8@+0x01, id9->u8@+0x05, id10->u8@+0x06. Do NOT route id8 through set_motion; the clip field +0x94 is written only by Plc_motion. Drop the RE2-id translation indirection for Member_set.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8004116c.c is opcode 0x34 (Member_set): dispatch table base PTR_LAB_800744a8 (ghidra1_V2.txt:243032), entry 0x80074578 = index (0x80074578-0x800744a8)/4 = 0x34, and its caller LAB_800410b8 (ghidra1_V2.txt:151264-151266) reads member_id=lbu 0x1(v0), value=lh 0x2(v0) and jal FUN_8004116c. The real per-id field writes (ghidra1_V2.txt:151340-151354): case6=sw a2,0x0(a0); case7=sw a2,0xc(a0); case8=sb a2,0x4(a0); case9=sb a2,0x5(a0); case10=sb a2,0x6(a0). Crucially the clip/animation field is +0x94: written ONLY by Plc_motion handler LAB_80041b90 `sb a1,0x94(v0)` (ghidra1_V2.txt:152132) and read by clip resolver FUN_8001f314 `lbu v0,0x94(t0)` (ghidra1_V2.txt:98298). Our code (psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/re15_to_re2.c:52-56) maps 6->0x06,7->0x07,8->0x08,9->0x09,10->0x0A; actor_common.c:137 routes 0x06->sub_state_4, has NO 0x07/0x09/0x0A case (dropped), and actor_common.c:138 routes 0x08->re15_actor_set_motion = sets the +0x94 clip (same helper used for ZB_CLIP_*/PLY_CLIP_* in zombie_common.c/player_damage_common.c). So id8 wrongly changes the animation instead of writing state byte +0x04, and id6/7/9/10 hit the wrong field or are silently dropped. Real usage proven: extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1040/scd/sub05.c:13-14 and room1041/scd/sub05.c:13-14 issue Member_set(9,15)/Member_set(10,0), both dropped by our 0x09/0x0A no-case.
verdict.fix_summary: Port FUN_8004116c directly for Member_set ids 6-10 instead of translating through re15_to_re2_member_id: id6->actor +0x00 (flags, s32), id7->+0x0c (s32), id8->u8 state byte +0x04 (NOT the clip; do NOT call re15_actor_set_motion), id9->u8 +0x05, id10->u8 +0x06. The animation clip (+0x94) is written exclusively by Plc_motion (LAB_80041b90), never by Member_set, so id8 must stop touching the clip. NOTE: the audit's stated offset for id8 was slightly off (it said +0x01; the real asm at 0x800411d8 is sb a2,0x4(a0) = +0x04) — fix to +0x04. Map these to the corresponding re15_actor_t fields (sub_state_1/state bytes) rather than the RE2-id indirection.

claim: Member_set id 12 (0x0C) is corrupted into a world-Y write. RE1.5 FUN_8004116c case 0xc writes a u8 to entity+0x09 (a state/flag byte). Our translate returns 0x0C unchanged (default 0x0B..0x25), actor_common case 0x0C = a->y. So room1020/1021/1070 Member_set(12,138) sets world Y=138 instead of the +0x09 flag. Likewise ids 0xB/0xD/0xE/0xF pass straight through to RE2 coordinate/rot cases that have NOTHING to do with the RE1.5 id meaning (RE1.5 id 0xB=+0x07, 0xD=+0x08, 0xE=+0x0a, 0xF=+0x0b per FUN_8004116c).
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8004116c.c:46-57 (case0xc=(int)param_1+9, case0xb=+7, case0xd=param_1+2=+0x08, case0xe=+0xa, case0xf=+0xb)
fix: Remove the 0x0B..0x25 default pass-through. Map every RE1.5 id through the FUN_8004116c table: 0xB->u8@+0x07, 0xC->u8@+0x09, 0xD->u8@+0x08, 0xE->u8@+0x0a, 0xF->u8@+0x0b, 0x10->s16@+0x1c4, 0x11->s16@+0x98, 0x12->u8@+0x82, 0x13->s16@+0x1ba.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8004116c.c:46-57 (case 0xc → *(u8*)((int)param_1+9); case 0xb → +7; case 0xd → *(u8*)(param_1+2)=byte+0x08; case 0xe → +0xa; case 0xf → +0xb). The RE1.5 Member_set opcode (dispatch entry 0x80074578 = handler LAB_800410b8) calls FUN_8004116c with the RAW RE1.5 member id and the LE s16 value: ghidra1_V2.txt:151257-151266 (lbu a1,0x1(v0) = member id; lh a2,0x2(v0) = value; jal FUN_8004116c; pc+=4). So FUN_8004116c's switch IS the canonical RE1.5 id→field map. Our path (scd_vm.c:2040-2042 op Member_set 0x34) does re15_to_re2_member_id(0xC) which hits the default 0x0B<=id<=0x25 pass-through (re15_to_re2.c:58-59) returning 0xC unchanged, then re15_actor_set_member(slot,0xC,138) → actor_common.c:140 a->y=value. Original writes a u8 to actor+0x09; ours writes the world-Y WORD. The opcode IS used: extracted/PSX/STAGE1/room1020/scd/sub03.c:7,10,13 + room1021/sub07 + room1070/sub02 + room1100/1101/1141 (Member_set(12,138) appears 153x). Pattern Work_set(2,N)+Member_set(12,138) = setting a per-entity state byte, NOT teleporting four actors to Y=138.
verdict.fix_summary: Real byte-true deviation, confirmed not refutable. Remove the 0x0B..0x25 default pass-through in re15_to_re2_member_id(). The pass-through is wrong because RE1.5 ids and RE2 ids are different namespaces: RE1.5 ids 0xB-0xF write small u8 state/flag bytes (FUN_8004116c: 0xB→+0x07, 0xC→+0x09, 0xD→+0x08(u8 via param_1+2), 0xE→+0x0a, 0xF→+0x0b), but pass-through routes them to RE2 coordinate/rotation cases (0xB→x, 0xC→y, 0xD→z, 0xE→rot_x, 0xF→rot_y). Map every RE1.5 id explicitly through the FUN_8004116c table to the corresponding RE2-struct state/flag field (a dedicated u8 field, never x/y/z/rot), e.g. 0xC must target a state byte and NOT a->y. Also note RE1.5 ids 0x10→s16@+0x1c4, 0x11→s16@+0x98, 0x12→u8@+0x82, 0x13→s16@+0x1ba — these too currently misroute (0x11→a->floor, 0x13→a->hp) or get dropped (0x12 absent from set_member). The width also differs: FUN_8004116c truncates 0xB-0xF/0x12 to u8 (uVar1) whereas our coordinate writes store the full 32-bit value. Verify each RE1.5 offset maps to the right RE2 field before wiring; do not blindly trust offsets if the actor struct layout differs.

claim: Member_set never writes the clip/motion field in the original. The motion_no byte at entity+0x94 is written EXCLUSIVELY by the Plc_motion handler (sb a1,0x94(v0)). FUN_8004116c has no case touching +0x94. Our mapping makes Member_set(8,..) call set_motion, i.e. any script using RE1.5 member-id 8 would swap the animation clip, which the engine does for a u8 at +0x01 instead.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:152132 (Plc_motion LAB_80041b90: sb a1,0x94(v0)) vs RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_8004116c.c (no +0x94 case; case8=(int)param_1+1)
fix: Member_set id8 must write a u8 to the +0x01 field, not call set_motion. Only Plc_motion (op_plc_motion) should call re15_actor_set_motion.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:151348 (FUN_8004116c caseD_8: `sb a2,0x4(a0)`) — Member_set id 8 writes a BYTE to the 0x154 work-object +0x04, NOT the motion/clip field. Caller LAB_800410b8 passes a0 = lw 0x154(s0) (ghidra1_V2.txt:151263), and the dispatch slot 0x80074578 = (0x80074578-0x800744a8)/4 = opcode 0x34 = Member_set. Plc_motion is a SEPARATE opcode 0x3F (slot 0x800745a4) whose handler LAB_80041b90 writes the motion_no to the SAME work-object at +0x94 (ghidra1_V2.txt:152132: `sb a1,0x94(v0)`, v0=lw 0x154(a0)). FUN_8004116c has NO case touching +0x94. Our actor_common.c:138 (case 0x08 -> re15_actor_set_motion) makes Member_set(8) swap the animation clip, which the original never does.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation (the claim's substance holds; its offset detail was slightly wrong — actual field is +0x04 on the 0x154 work-object, not +0x01 on the entity). Original FUN_8004116c case 8 = `sb a2,0x4(a0)` writes a u8 STATE byte to the work-object +0x04; it does NOT touch the motion clip at +0x94. The clip is written EXCLUSIVELY by Plc_motion (opcode 0x3F, LAB_80041b90, `sb a1,0x94(v0)`). FIX: actor_common.c:138 must NOT call re15_actor_set_motion for member id 0x08; it should write a u8 to the actor's state field that corresponds to work-object +0x04 (in our struct that is the field Plc_motion sets to 4, i.e. the locomotion/run-state marker — same field as case 0x04's group). Only op_plc_motion should ever call re15_actor_set_motion. Also drop the re15_to_re2_member_id `case 8 -> 0x08 /* motion */` clip aliasing. Note: room1170 never executes Member_set(8) (it uses Plc_motion for animation), so this is latent for other rooms — verify which rooms fire Member_set with id 8 before/while fixing.

claim: mat3_from_euler accumulates the two terms of each off-diagonal element in full Q36 precision and shifts ONCE (e.g. m[3] = ((sz*cx<<12)+(cz*sy*sx))>>24). The original game's RotMatrix truncates each product to 12 fractional bits SEPARATELY (and the triple-product cz*sy*sx is truncated TWICE: once after cz*sy, once after *sx), then sums the already-truncated short terms. The 'M-round 2026-05-25' comment frames the single-shift as a precision 'fix', but it provably does NOT reproduce the original's per-product/double truncation, so the resulting matrix elements differ by up to ~1-2 LSB from the real engine. This is the kind of sub-LSB matrix drift consistent with the user-reported recurring pixel shift.
disasm_cite: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/RotMatrix.c:34-39 (iVar4 = iVar5*iVar9 >> 0xc;  m[1][0] = (short)(iVar7*iVar3>>0xc) - (short)(iVar4*iVar6>>0xc); etc.) and ghidra1_V2.txt:210107-210167 (each multu followed by its own `sra t,t,0xc` and `sh` store, two already-shifted shorts then addu/subu)
fix: Replicate the original per-product truncation exactly. For each off-diagonal: pre-truncate the inner product first (iv = (cz*sy)>>12 with arithmetic shift), then m = ((sz*cx)>>12) +/- ((iv*sx)>>12). Do NOT pre-shift the single products into Q36 and combine. Equivalently, port the game RotMatrix arithmetic verbatim using s16-truncated intermediates (matching RE_15_Quellcode_V2/RotMatrix.c:18-39), or on PSX call a RotMatrix that matches the game's order (NOT PSn00bSDK's RotMatrix, which builds Rx*Ry*Rz).

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:210106-210167 (each off-diagonal element: multu -> mflo -> `sra t,t,0xc` -> a SECOND multu -> mflo -> its own `sra t,t,0xc` -> `addu`/`subu` of the two ALREADY-shifted 16-bit values -> `sh` store; e.g. m[1][0]@0x6: 80068240 multu;80068250 sra,0xc (term A);80068258 multu;80068268 sra,0xc (term B);8006826c subu;80068274 sh) and RE_15_Quellcode_V2/RotMatrix.c:34-39 (iVar4 = iVar5*iVar9 >> 0xc; then m[1][0] = (short)(iVar7*iVar3>>0xc) - (short)(iVar4*iVar6>>0xc) — each product cast to short and >>0xc SEPARATELY before the sub; the triple product is truncated TWICE: once into iVar4, once at *iVar6>>0xc). Our code at psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/skeleton_common.c:138-160 instead does t1=((int64_t)sz*cx)<<12; t2=(int64_t)cz*sy*sx; m[3]=(t1+t2)>>24 — full Q36 accumulation, single shift, NO per-product/double truncation. Live caller at skeleton_common.c:358 (per-bone pose matrix).
verdict.fix_summary: Replicate the original per-product truncation exactly instead of the single-shift Q36 accumulation. For each off-diagonal element, pre-truncate the inner double product first: e.g. for m[3]: iv = (int32_t)(short)(((int32_t)cz*sy)>>12); then m[3] = (int32_t)(short)(((int32_t)sz*cx)>>12) + (int32_t)(short)((iv*sx)>>12). Apply the same short-truncate-each-term pattern to m[4],m[6],m[7] (the four 2-term off-diagonals), matching RotMatrix.c:34-39 arithmetic (use 32-bit ints with arithmetic >>0xc and a (short) cast per product, then add/sub the two shorts). Do NOT keep the Q36 t1<<12 + t2 single >>24 form — that is the deviation. On PSX, equivalently call a RotMatrix that builds Ry*Rx*Rz with this exact truncation order (NOT PSn00bSDK's RotMatrix). The 'M-round 2026-05-25' comment that frames the single-shift as a precision fix is wrong relative to byte-true: it diverges from the original up to ~1-2 LSB per element, drifting bone-transformed vertices and producing the recurring pixel shift.

claim: PSX backend uses PSn00bSDK isin/icos. The original RE1.5 used its own table DAT_800794c4 (PsyQ rcossin layout). Whether PSn00bSDK's isin/icos return bit-identical values to that table at every angle is unverified; if they differ by an LSB the whole pose chain drifts.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:263165 (DAT_800794c4 cos/sin table xref'd by RotMatrix) ; ghidra1_V2.txt:209990-209994 (low16->sin via sll/sra 0x10, high16->cos via sra 0x10)
fix: Dump DAT_800794c4 from PSX.EXE (4096 entries x 4 bytes at the resolved address) and compare element-by-element against PSn00bSDK isin/icos; if any LSB differs, replace re15_sin_q12/re15_cos_q12 with a direct read of the game's extracted table.

verdict.proof: PSn00bSDK isin source = polynomial approx, NOT the game's table: info/Resident_Evil_und_Playstation_Information/PSn00bSDK-60deeff/PSn00bSDK-master/libpsn00b/psxgte/isin.c:11-28 (isin = coranac isin_S4: y = B - x^2*C, B=19900 C=3516; icos:30-34 = isin(x+1024)). The original RE1.5 trig is the lookup table DAT_800794c4 (4096 entries, 4 bytes each, sin=low16/cos=high16) xref'd by RotMatrixX/Y/Z, RotMatrixYXZ and the per-bone matrix builders FGO_01_OBJ/FGO_02_OBJ: ghidra1_V2.txt:263165-263182 (xref list) and the read+unpack code ghidra1_V2.txt:209988-209994 (lw t9=>DAT_800794c4; sll/sra 0x10 = low16 sin; sra 0x10 = high16 cos). Decoded table bytes at ghidra1_V2.txt:263183-263251 vs PSn00bSDK isin/icos differ at the LSB in the first 17 entries: idx1 sin 6 vs 7; idx4 sin 25 vs 26; idx5 sin 31 vs 32; idx8 50 vs 51; idx11 69 vs 70; idx12 75 vs 76; idx15 94 vs 95; idx10-15 cos 4095 vs 4096. Our PSX backend = isin/icos (psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/skeleton_trig_psx.c:10-11); our PC backend = sinf/cosf truncated (psx_dev/re15_reborn_pc/src/skeleton_trig_pc.c:17-29) — a third, also-divergent method. Both feed the skeleton matrix builders skeleton_common.c:85-128. Neither reproduces DAT_800794c4 bit-for-bit.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation, not engine-specific. Extract the 4096-entry table DAT_800794c4 from PSX.EXE (4096 words at 0x800794c4; each word: low16 = sin signed, high16 = cos signed, Q12 with 4096=1.0) and back both re15_sin_q12/re15_cos_q12 by direct table reads (sin = table[angle & 0xfff] low16; cos = table[angle & 0xfff] high16, matching the disasm's andi 0xfff + sll/sra 0x10 unpack at ghidra1_V2.txt:209983-209994). Replace BOTH backends: PSX skeleton_trig_psx.c (drop isin/icos polynomial) and PC skeleton_trig_pc.c (drop sinf/cosf). This makes the per-bone pose chain bit-identical to the original since the same table feeds RotMatrix*/FGO_*_OBJ. Verify: dump the table once and assert it round-trips the negation path (subu zero,angle for negative angles) used at 209981.

claim: RE15_INV_MAX_SLOTS is hardcoded to 11 with the comment 'DAT_800d46ac latches the live count between 8 and 0xB'. The real EXE inventory has NO 11-slot configuration and DAT_800d46ac is not the count. The slot count lives in DAT_800b0fbc and the engine branches on it being exactly 8 or 0xa (10). The '11 / 0xB' value is a guess; the count is also DYNAMIC (loaded from the save block), not a fixed compile-time constant.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:160363 (lbu v1,DAT_800b0fbc) + :160364 (ori v0,0xa) + :160366 (ori v0,0x8) — renderer FUN_80047648 dispatches purely on count==0xa vs ==8; also ghidra1_V2.txt:160415-160417 (count==8 then ==0xa). DAT_800d46ac is uninitialized BSS: ghidra1_V2.txt:640245 (800d46ac ?? ??).
fix: Replace the fixed 11 with the byte-true dynamic count: store the live slot count (DAT_800b0fbc) as a runtime field initialized to 8 (default) and switchable to 0xa (10) when the side-pack is present. The hard upper bound is 10, not 11 (FUN_80048704 gates slot index with sltu against DAT_800b0fbc at ghidra1_V2.txt:161427-161429). If a static array is kept, size it 10 and never reference index 10 as a valid slot.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:160363-160366 (lbu v1,DAT_800b0fbc; ori v0,0xa; bne; ori v0,0x8 — count branched ONLY on 0xa vs 8); ghidra1_V2.txt:161427-161429 (lbu v0,DAT_800b0fbc; andi a0,0xff; sltu v0,a0,v0 — slot index gated < count); ghidra1_V2.txt:640245 (800d46ac ?? ?? = uninitialized BSS) — and DAT_800d46ac has ZERO xrefs in the entire listing (only its own BSS byte), so it cannot be the count; ghidra1_V2.txt:109842 (DAT_800b0fbc passed as base to copy-routine FUN_8004ee38 = loaded from save block, dynamic not constant). All 19 DAT_800b0fbc comparisons (160414/160933/160984/161134/161185/161527/161628 etc.) test only 8 or 0xa — no 0xb anywhere.
verdict.fix_summary: Could NOT refute — the audit is correct. (1) The header comment 'DAT_800d46ac latches the live count between 8 and 0xB' is fabricated: DAT_800d46ac is unreferenced BSS (ghidra1_V2.txt:640245, zero xrefs) and is NOT the count; no 0xB exists anywhere. The real count is DAT_800b0fbc (19 xrefs), loaded from the save block (109842) and branched on EXACTLY 8 (default) or 0xa=10 (side-pack). (2) The fixed 11 is wrong — max valid count is 10, max valid slot index 9; the slot-index access is gated with sltu against the count (161429), so the original rejects index>=count. Our code uses RE15_INV_MAX_SLOTS=11 as the grant loop/array bound (inventory_common.c:26,36), so an item CAN land in slot 10 (11th), a slot the original treats as out-of-bounds — a real (latent) behavioral deviation. FIX: store the live count as a runtime field init to 8, switchable to 10 (0xa) with the side-pack; size any static array to 10; never treat index 10 as valid; delete the fabricated DAT_800d46ac/0xB comment. Note: deviation is currently latent (no shipped room uses inventory per the header), so visually neutral today — but the constant and its justifying comment are both provably false.

claim: re15_inv_grant uses a generic 'stack onto first same-id slot, else first free slot' policy. The real add routine (FUN_8004dc4c) does NOT do same-id stacking; it splits on item class: non-stackable items (where (byte)(id-0x0E) > 5) go to the first FREE slot via the free-slot search FUN_8004df2c; stackable items are inserted at slot 0 by right-shifting ALL slots and creating a paired double-wide slot (flags 1 at first half, 2 at second half). Our 'merge same id qty += amount' path has no counterpart in the original add path and the 'first free slot' search must use the original DAT_800b0fbc-bounded loop.
disasm_cite: RE_15_modified_V2/inventory_add_item.c:26 (5 < (byte)(param_1-0xeU) class split) + :30 (FUN_8004df2c free-slot) + :40-95 (shift-all + paired double slot, flags 1/2); real free-slot search loop bounded by DAT_800b0fbc at ghidra1_V2.txt:168629-168654 (FUN_8004df2c: a0=DAT_800b0fbc, scan id!=0).
fix: Implement the two real paths: compute class = (uint8_t)(type - 0x0E) > 5. If non-stackable: find first slot in [0, count) with id==0 (loop bound = live count DAT_800b0fbc, not RE15_INV_MAX_SLOTS) and write id/qty/flags=0. If stackable: right-shift slots and create the paired double slot per FUN_8004dc4c (DAT_800b25c8 count += 2, flags 1/2). Drop the same-id qty-merge unless a disasm path for it is found (the cited add path has none).

verdict.proof: RE_15_modified_V2/inventory_add_item.c:26 (class split `5 < (byte)(param_1-0xeU)`), :28-37 (non-stackable -> FUN_8004df2c first-free + write id/qty/flags=0), :38-96 (stackable -> right-shift ALL slots + new paired double-slot at pos0, flags 1/2, DAT_800b25c8+=2); free-slot search FUN_8004df2c bounded by live count DAT_800b0fbc at ghidra1_V2.txt:168629-168654 (a0=DAT_800b0fbc, scan DAT_800b10ac[v1*4] for id==0); real add routine called once with (id,qty,gfx) at ghidra1_V2.txt:96383 (jal FUN_8004dc4c) with no same-id merge wrapper. Our code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/inventory_common.c:26-34 (Pass-1 same-id qty+=amount merge — NO counterpart in original) and :36-44 (Pass-2 free-slot bound = RE15_INV_MAX_SLOTS, not DAT_800b0fbc). Our justifying comment cites FUN_8006947c which does NOT exist in ghidra1_V2.txt (grep: 0 hits).
verdict.fix_summary: Replace re15_inv_grant's same-id-merge + free-slot logic with the two real FUN_8004dc4c paths. Compute class = (uint8_t)(type-0x0E) > 5. Non-stackable (class true): find first slot with id==0 within the LIVE count bound (port of DAT_800b0fbc, not RE15_INV_MAX_SLOTS), write id=type/qty=amount/flags=0 (FUN_8004df2c). Stackable (class false): right-shift all slots and insert a paired double-slot at slot 0 (first half flags=1, second half flags=2, count += 2) per FUN_8004dc4c:40-96. Remove the Pass-1 same-id qty-merge (no disasm counterpart) and delete the bogus FUN_8006947c comment cite. Caveat: if the reborn engine deliberately uses a flat single-slot model (no 2-wide paired cells), the paired-double-slot part may be a justified engine_specific simplification — but the same-id qty-merge and the RE15_INV_MAX_SLOTS bound are still provable deviations to fix regardless.

claim: qty merge 'qty = qty + amount' with no clamp is annotated as matching FUN_8006947c raw write. FUN_8006947c is not an inventory function — it is a 'beq v0,zero' branch inside an unrelated routine, so the cite does not support the claim. The actual slot writes (id/qty/flags) are the byte stores in FUN_8004dc4c.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:211883 (8006947c beq v0,zero,SYS_OBJ_ED0) — a mid-function branch, not a slot writer. Real slot writes: RE_15_modified_V2/inventory_add_item.c:34-36 ((&DAT_800b10ac)[iVar3]=id ; [+1]=qty ; [+2]=0) and ghidra1_V2.txt:168478-168482.
fix: Re-cite the qty/raw-write behavior to FUN_8004dc4c (id=+0, qty=+1, flags=+2). No qty clamp is present in that add path, so 'no 255 clamp' is fine — but the FUN_8006947c reference in the comment is wrong and should be removed/corrected. The same-id merge itself still needs a real disasm source (see grant finding).

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:168422 (FUN_8004dc4c entry, the real add-item) + 168443-168446 (addiu v0,s0,-0xe / andi 0xff / sltiu v0,0x6 / beq -> stackable-branch select) + 168402-168410 (free-slot branch raw writes id/qty/flags to DAT_800b10ac/ad/ae) + 168547-168557 (stackable branch inserts new item at slot0 after full slot rotation, raw qty=param_2). Bogus cite confirmed bogus at ghidra1_V2.txt:211883 (8006947c beq v0,zero,SYS_OBJ_ED0 -- a branch in an unrelated SYS_OBJ_* routine, not an inventory writer). Our code: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/inventory_common.c:28 (qty = qty + amount same-id merge).
verdict.fix_summary: Two deviations vs the real add path FUN_8004dc4c. (1) CITE: remove the FUN_8006947c reference in the inventory_common.c:22-25 comment (proven non-inventory branch at ghidra1_V2.txt:211883); re-pin to FUN_8004dc4c (ghidra1_V2.txt:168422). (2) BEHAVIOR: our Pass-1 same-id qty merge (line 28 qty+=amount) does NOT exist in the original. FUN_8004dc4c never accumulates onto an existing slot: items with (uint8)(id-0x0E)<6 (IDs 0x0E..0x13, stackable) rotate ALL slots right and insert a fresh double-slot at position 0 with the raw qty=param_2; all other ids go to a free slot (FUN_8004df2c) with raw id/qty/flags=0. To be byte-true, replace the same-id merge with: stackable-id (0x0E..0x13) -> slot rotation + insert-at-0; else -> first-free-slot raw write. The field layout (id=+0,qty=+1,flags=+2,stride 4) is already byte-true (matches DAT_800b10ac writes). 'No 255 clamp' is correct (raw byte store), so keep that. NOTE: the project's confirmed inventory model is the 4-byte/11-slot table (per MEMORY byte_true_hack_audit #14); the rotation/double-slot scheme here is the EXE's own layout -- reconcile with the engine's chosen 11-slot model before porting, but the same-id-merge is a deviation either way.

claim: re15_dialog_open hardcodes message_scroll=2 and message_timer=1 with the comment 'DAT_800b8524 default: 2 frames per glyph'. The comment/value is WRONG: the original sets scroll = 2<<bVar2 and timer = 1<<bVar2 where bVar2 = (DAT_800b5456==0). For ordinary in-game dialog DAT_800b5456 is 0 at gameplay init, so bVar2=1 → scroll default = 2<<1 = 4 frames/glyph and timer init = 1<<1 = 2. Our typewriter therefore reveals glyphs ~2x too fast.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:112759-112767 (LAB_80028194: DAT_800b8525 = 1<<s1; DAT_800b8524 = 2<<s1) with s1 from ghidra1_V2.txt:112735,112743 (bVar2 = sltiu s1,v0,1 = (DAT_800b5456==0)); gameplay value DAT_800b5456=0 at ghidra1_V2.txt:82440 (FUN_800161e0 sb zero,DAT_800b5456)
fix: Derive the shift like the original: bVar2 = (DAT_800b5456==0) → for gameplay messages bVar2=1. Set message_scroll = 2<<1 = 4 and message_timer = 1<<1 = 2 in re15_dialog_open (or carry the buffer flag and shift). Fix the comment to 'DAT_800b8524 default = 2<<bVar2 = 4 for normal single-buffer messages'.

verdict.proof: ghidra1_V2.txt:112735 (lbu v0,DAT_800b5456) + 112743 (sltiu s1,v0,0x1 => s1=bVar2=(DAT_800b5456==0)); ghidra1_V2.txt:112759-112761 (ori v0,1; sllv v0,v0,s1; sb v0,DAT_800b8525 => timer=1<<bVar2); ghidra1_V2.txt:112762-112767 (ori v0,2; sllv v0,v0,s1; sb v0,DAT_800b8524 => scroll=2<<bVar2). Confirmed in decompile RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80028134.c:14,19,20 (bVar2=DAT_800b5456=='\0'; DAT_800b8525=1<<bVar2; DAT_800b8524=2<<bVar2). DAT_800b5456 is a display-mode flag (consumed by FUN_80061fc0/GPU_REG1 at ghidra1_V2.txt:101042) set to 0 in the in-game room-render state FUN_800460b8 (ghidra1_V2.txt:158690, table entry @80074bdc) and to 2 only in full-screen menu modes (ghidra1_V2.txt:100322,110201,159104). Therefore in-game DAT_800b5456=0 => bVar2=1 => scroll=2<<1=4, timer=1<<1=2. Our msg_common.c:339-340 hardcodes scroll=2,timer=1 (the bVar2=0 menu-mode values).
verdict.fix_summary: In re15_dialog_open (msg_common.c:339-340), for normal in-game dialog set message_scroll = 4 (=2<<1) and message_timer = 2 (=1<<1), matching DAT_800b8524=2<<bVar2 / DAT_800b8525=1<<bVar2 with bVar2=(DAT_800b5456==0)=1 in the room-render state. Ideally carry the display-mode flag and derive the shift (bVar2 = (display_mode==0)) so menu-mode dialog (flag==2 -> bVar2=0) still gets 2/1; but the current flat 2/1 is the wrong (menu) branch for free-roam dialog, making the typewriter ~2x too fast and ignoring the <<bVar2 scaling applied throughout FUN_80028134 (e.g. FUN_80028134.c:52,61,86,120,131,140,156). Also correct the comment to 'DAT_800b8524 default = 2<<bVar2 = 4 for in-game (DAT_800b5456==0)'.

claim: Typewriter fast-forward uses g_scd_action_held = SQUARE button (RE15_PAD_BIT_SQUARE=0x8000, set in game_step_common.c:40). The original gates fast-forward on DAT_800ac768 & 0x4000, which our own header maps to CROSS (RE15_PAD_BIT_CROSS=0x4000), not SQUARE. So the original speeds the typewriter with a different physical button than our port.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:112786-112788 (lw v0,DAT_800ac768; andi v0,v0,0x4000) = FUN_80028134.c:29 ((DAT_800ac768 & 0x4000)!=0); bit identity in our re15_player.h:41 (CROSS=0x4000)
fix: If the engine pad-bit numbering matches the original PSX pad word, change the fast-forward (and confirm) to read the 0x4000/CROSS bit instead of SQUARE. This may be intentionally remapped at the SDL/input layer (engine_specific) — confirm whether the original 0x4000 corresponds to our CROSS before changing.

verdict.proof: Original fast-forward gate = bit 0x4000: ghidra1_V2.txt:112786-112788 (lw v0,DAT_800ac768; andi v0,v0,0x4000) decompiled at RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80028134.c:29 ((DAT_800ac768 & 0x4000)!=0 -> step decrements by 4 = fast-forward, state 1 TYPING). DAT_800ac768 is the held BIOS pad word (reset to 0xffff on confirm at FUN_80028134.c:127). Bit identity: 0x4000 = Cross per PSn00bSDK psxpad.h:36 (PAD_CROSS = 1<<14) and our own re15_player.h:41 (RE15_PAD_BIT_CROSS=0x4000); 0x8000 = Square (psxpad.h:37 / re15_player.h:42). Our PSX input reads the SAME unmodified PADTYPE.btn layout into pad_current (psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/input.c:24-30, ~pad->btn), so 0x4000 is physically Cross on both sides (NOT remapped at the input layer). Yet our gate binds the held action to SQUARE: psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/game_step_common.c:40 (g_scd_action_held = (c->pad_current & RE15_PAD_BIT_SQUARE)), consumed at psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/msg_common.c:365,370 (act_held -> dec=4). => original fast-forwards with Cross, our port with Square.
verdict.fix_summary: In psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/game_step_common.c:40 change the held-fast-forward bind from RE15_PAD_BIT_SQUARE (0x8000) to RE15_PAD_BIT_CROSS (0x4000) so g_scd_action_held = (c->pad_current & RE15_PAD_BIT_CROSS). The input layer is the unmodified PSX pad word on both sides, so this maps to the same physical Cross button the original FUN_80028134 reads. Note: this fix is ONLY for the typewriter fast-forward LEVEL (g_scd_action_held). The EDGE/advance-confirm path (g_aot_action_pressed at game_step_common.c:27, still Square) is a separate question and was NOT proven a deviation here -- verify the advance/confirm button against the original's confirm gates (e.g. FUN_80028134.c:144,164 use 0x4000/0xc000) before touching it.

claim: On fast-forward the original reveals up to TWO glyphs per frame (iVar8=2 set when held+scroll<4; the LAB_80028434 'if s2!=0 goto LAB_80028250' loops the glyph emit twice). Our TYPING case reveals exactly one glyph per frame (the for(;;) breaks after one reveal) and only subtracts 4 from the timer. The reveal rate during hold-to-skip is therefore not byte-true (1 glyph/frame instead of 2).
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:112793-112797 (addiu v1,v1,-4; ori s2,zero,2 = iVar8=2 on held+DAT_800b8524<4) and ghidra1_V2.txt:112954 (bne s2,zero,LAB_80028250 → re-process a 2nd glyph) = FUN_80028134.c:29-31,40-42
fix: When act_held && message_scroll<4, allow processing two glyphs in one frame (loop the reveal twice, matching iVar8=2), in addition to the dec=4 timer subtraction.

verdict.proof: RE_15_Quellcode_V2/FUN_80028134.c:29-31 (held 0x4000 flag + DAT_800b8524<4 → bVar5=DAT_800b8525-4 AND iVar8=2) and FUN_80028134.c:40,187-192 (LAB_80028250 emits a glyph + iVar8--, LAB_80028434: `if (iVar8 == 0) goto LAB_80028748; goto LAB_80028250` = process a 2nd glyph). Disasm: ghidra1_V2.txt:112793 (addiu v1,v1,-0x4 = timer-=4), 112794 (sltiu v0,v0,0x4 = DAT_800b8524<4), 112797 (ori s2,zero,0x2 = iVar8=2), 112809 (addiu s2,s2,-0x1 at LAB_80028250 = budget--), 112954 (bne s2,zero,LAB_80028250 = loop a 2nd glyph). Our code psx_dev/re15_reborn/src/msg_common.c:373-397: the for(;;) reveals exactly ONE glyph then break (line 394-396); dec=4 (line 370) only drains message_timer, never emits a 2nd glyph. Variable mapping verified: DAT_800b8525=message_timer (FUN_80028134.c:26,36 per-glyph countdown), DAT_800b8524=message_scroll (FUN_80028134.c:86 set by 0x04 opcode, our 0x04 handler msg_common.c:392). Default scroll=2 (<4, msg_common.c:339) so the 2-glyph path is the NORMAL fast-forward case, not an edge case.
verdict.fix_summary: Real deviation, fix it. When act_held && message_scroll<4, the original (a) drains the per-glyph timer by 4 (we already do, dec=4) AND (b) sets a 2-glyph emit budget iVar8=2 so up to TWO printable glyphs are revealed in one frame (FUN_80028134.c:30 + LAB_80028434 loop-back at :191-192). Our TYPING for(;;) breaks after a single reveal, so on hold-to-skip we type at 1 glyph/frame instead of 2 — visibly half the original fast-forward speed (the timer-drain change alone does NOT compensate). Byte-true fix: give the reveal loop a budget (2 when held&&scroll<4, else 1); each time a PRINTABLE glyph is revealed, decrement the budget and continue the loop if budget>0 (mirroring LAB_80028250 s2-- and LAB_80028434 `if(iVar8==0)done else goto LAB_80028250`); control codes (page-break/end/select/color/scroll/newline) still break/continue as now. Note: the budget is consumed per printable glyph emit, not a blanket "run the body twice", so emitting two should stop at a control code exactly like the original.

claim: The TYPING 0x04 handler does 'a=raw[parse+1]; if(a) scroll=a; parse+=2' — when the arg is 0 we leave scroll unchanged and skip 2 bytes. The original's 0x04 with arg 0 runs a forward SCAN: it walks past following codes until the NEXT 0x04 and uses that 0x04's argument as the new scroll speed. Our code ignores the scan entirely.
disasm_cite: ghidra1_V2.txt:112870-112911 (caseD_4: bne v0,zero — if arg==0 loops LAB_8002835c scanning for the next 0x04, then sb v0,DAT_800b8524) = FUN_80028134.c:70-87 (case 4 while-loop until *pbVar7==4)
fix: Implement the arg==0 scan: from parse+2, walk control codes (2-byte: 5/6/9/A/B; 1-byte others) until the next 0x04, take its argument <<bVar2 as the new scroll, and advance parse to just past it. Rare in shipped .msg but provably divergent.
